v1.fact.network_config_database_postgresql16_200_conn_src_json_dt_2026_03_09: PostgreSQL 16 | max connections 200 | backup every 6 hours | retention 90 days

v1.fact.hybrid_scorer_uses_max_pooling_bm25_plus_ngram_embedding: HybridScorer combines BM25 keyword matching and character n-gram cosine similarity via max-pooling strategy. Default embeddingScale=0.8, convergenceBoost=0.1.

v1.fact.embedding_provider_interface_pluggable_for_model2vec: EmbeddingProvider interface decouples scoring from embedding algorithm. NgramHashEmbedding ships now; Model2Vec can be swapped in via the same interface when available.

v1.event.model2vec_semantic_embeddings_integrated_dt_2026_03_12: Model2Vec 29528-token 256-dim static embeddings integrated into MMPM hybrid search. Semantic R@1 jumped from 28.6% to 71.4%. Overall MRR from 0.647 to 0.918 (+41.9%).
v1.procedure.always_specify_cmd_context = When giving terminal commands to user ALWAYS specify which machine and which user and which droplet. User correction from 2026-03-14.

v1.procedure.cowork_vm_cannot_npm_install = The Cowork VM cannot run npm install due to filesystem permissions on mounted folders. The user installs dependencies on their Mac when needed. Always ask the user to run npm install/ci locally.

v1.procedure.we_work_as_a_team = We work as a team. The user handles commands on their Mac and droplets. Claude handles code analysis, writing fixes, and giving clear instructions specifying WHERE to run each command. Never assume Claude can do everything alone.

v1.event.website_first_deploy_dt_2026_03_14 = parametric-memory.dev first successful deploy to production. CI passed (lint, typecheck, format, test, build) then Deploy workflow ran Docker build on droplet 170.64.238.232. Site live with SSL.
v1.fact.next_env_dts_must_be_committed = next-env.d.ts must be committed to git (not gitignored) for tsc --noEmit to work in CI. Contains Next.js type references needed by tsconfig.json.

v1.event.droplet_ops_skill_created_dt_2026_03_14 = Created and installed droplet-ops skill in Cowork. Covers droplet setup phases (users, software, firewall, SSL, deploy, CI/CD verify, harden), memory substrate blacklist (mmpm.co.nz / 170.64.198.144), SSH Mac setup, and team workflow patterns. Packaged via skill-creator package_skill.py.

v1.procedure.ssh_passphrase_handling = SSH keys should keep their passphrases for security. Use ssh-add to cache in agent and UseKeychain yes in SSH config for Mac persistence. Do not advise passphrase removal.

v1.state.sprint_v1_needs_mmpm_server_codebase = Steps 1.2-1.6 require MMPM server source code mounted in workspace. Key file mmpm_mcp_http.ts. Cannot implement without seeing actual auth middleware and handler code.

v1.state.sprint_v1_proxy_only_approach = Proxy-only client mode chosen over server-side changes. Website API proxy exposes 10 read-safe endpoints, never proxies writes. Bearer held server-side. Zero MMPM server changes. Server-side OAuth2 scopes deferred to Sprint V4 for real-time streaming.

v1.fact.mmpm_api_surface_27_tools_audited = 27 MCP tools audited. 10 read-safe for viz proxy. 17 write or admin never proxied. Minor side-effects from search and access acceptable on 57-atom tree with occasional viz queries.

v1.fact.viz_file_structure = src/lib/mmpm.ts (proxy client), src/types/memory.ts (response types), src/stores/memory-store.ts (Zustand), src/app/api/memory/[...path]/route.ts (catch-all proxy), src/app/visualise/page.tsx (dynamic import), src/components/visualise/ (6 R3F components)

v1.fact.adr_001_sse_readonly_temporal_connections = ADR for OAuth2 read scope plus SSE event stream plus temporal connection visualization. Removes master key from website. Read scope strips side-effects. SSE batches changesets per commit. White temporal lines connect batch members in 3D viz. ~728 lines across server and website.

v1.fact.temporal_ghost_persistence = Temporal lines persist as ghosts fading linearly over 5 minutes. Ghost phase starts at 4.3s with 15% opacity decaying to 0% at 5min. Groups garbage-collected at 300s. Shows pattern of recent history as fading web.
v1.fact.changeset_replay_deferred = Timeline slider for changeset replay deferred to future sprint. Ghost persistence partially addresses the need.

v1.fact.fastify_sse_reply_raw_write = Fastify SSE uses reply.raw.writeHead then reply.raw.write for streaming. Must set reply.sent true to prevent Fastify auto-closing. Clean up via reply.raw.on close.

v1.procedure.changeset_atom_index_is_leaf_not_sorted = SSE changeset index is leaf index in shard Merkle tree not BFS sorted position. Must re-sort and re-layout entire shard after adding atoms via SSE. requestLayoutDebounced handles this.

v1.fact.proxy_security_fix = mmpm-website src/lib/mmpm.ts loadFunction only reads viz var. No fallback to master. If missing logs warning and sends no auth so server returns 401. Prevents silent privilege escalation.

v1.fact.live_integration_test_58_pass = tools/harness/live_scope_test.sh runs 58 tests against local server covering master checkpoint flow and read-only write blocks and zero mutation proof and audit trail. All passing.

v1.fact.mmpm_github_workflows_three = Three GitHub CI workflows in .github/workflows/: (1) readiness-fast-tests.yml (push+PR to main, tests+typecheck+bench), (2) bench-ci-api.yml (manual dispatch, health-gated API benchmark), (3) mcp-semantic-gate.yml (push+PR, MCP tool tests)
v1.fact.vscode_mcp_config_oauth_only = VS Code mcp.json uses bare type http with URL only and VS Code handles OAuth PKCE automatically via 401 WWW-Authenticate discovery
v1.event.deployed_session_resilience_2026_03_16 = Deployed stale session tolerance and TTL reaper to mmpm.co.nz production MCP server
v1.event.sse_simulation_test_dt_2026_03_17 = SSE simulation test run demonstrating all animation types
v1.fact.sse_animation_types_four = Four SSE animation types: add cascade (cyan), tombstone cascade (pink), train arc (indigo), access pulse (amber)
v1.fact.sse_diagnostic_probe = Atom added during SSE diagnostic test to verify commit broadcast

v1.fact.sse_commit_event_not_implemented = MMPM server /events SSE endpoint emits connected access and clients events but does NOT emit commit events. The commit flush codepath has no SSE broadcast wired in. This blocks add cascade tombstone cascade and train arc animations on the Substrate Viewer
v1.fact.visualiser_camera_director_removed = CameraDirector.tsx deleted, all automated camera and structure rotation code removed. User controls view via OrbitControls only.
v1.fact.visualiser_dimming_removed = Atom dimming during animations removed for GPU performance. Color buffer only uploads when affected atoms actually change color.
v1.fact.server_monolith_3018_lines = server.ts is 3018 lines handling 30 endpoints, SSE, auth, metrics, search ranking. Top refactor priority is splitting into route modules.
v1.fact.sse_event_types_four = Four SSE event types: connected (handshake), commit (batched add/tombstone/train mutations), access (immediate atom lookup pulse), clients (subscriber count update)

v1.fact.sse_emitting_operations = Only mutation operations emit SSE events: session_checkpoint (commit event with add/tombstone/train), memory_access with master key (access event). Read-only operations (memory_search, memory_session_bootstrap, memory_context) emit nothing.

v1.fact.visualiser_animations_six_components = Six animation components: AtomNodes (flash colors + sequential growth), MerkleRehashCascade (add/tombstone descent + rehash + access descent), TrainParticles (bezier arc lightning), SseEventHighlight (fallback for unknown types), Effects (bloom), BackgroundParticles
v1.event.compute_platform_planned_dt_2026_03_19 = Pay-per-second compute platform designed and planned into 9 sprints S2-S10 with 74 testable items
v1.state.sprint1_dns_hsts_complete = Sprint 1 nearly done DNS records added HSTS preloaded security headers green Resend verification pending
v1.fact.compute_pause_snapshot_poweroff_14d = Pause creates snapshot and powers off droplet 14 day retention then full destruction
v1.fact.compute_management_plane_dedicated = Management plane runs on dedicated droplet separate from customer workloads and mmpm server

v1.state.sprint2_database_foundation_complete = Sprint 2 all 12 items coded: project scaffold, Docker Compose with PG16, 6 SQL migrations for all tables, connection pool and health check, seed data with edge cases, schema verification integration test with Testcontainers, pool unit tests. Awaiting local npm install to run.
v1.fact.mmpm_compute_migration_tooling = Uses node-pg-migrate for CLI and custom src/db/migrate.ts for programmatic use in tests via Testcontainers
v1.state.sprint2_needs_npm_install = Sprint 2 code complete but npm install blocked in sandbox. User must run npm install and npm run test:integration locally to validate

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_production_db_digitalocean = Production database for mmpm-compute will be a managed DigitalOcean PostgreSQL cluster not Docker. Docker PG is dev only. Need physical backups and a good snapshot policy.

v1.state.sprint3_accounts_stripe_complete = Sprint 3 all 8 items coded: account service CRUD, billing events service (append-only), credit service (atomic tx + idempotent on stripe_payment_intent_id), Stripe Checkout endpoint, Stripe webhook handler with sig verification, balance query API with burn rate calc, billing events query API with pagination, property-based balance invariant tests with fast-check. Express app at port 3100.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_test_structure = Three test configs: vitest (default unused), vitest.integration (tests/integration + tests/unit with Testcontainers), vitest.property (tests/property with fast-check). npm run test:all runs all three.
v1.event.sprint4_complete_dt_2026_03_19 = Sprint 4 API management completed and coded. Awaiting test run on local machine.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_billing_model_per_minute = Billing model changed from per-second to per-minute as of 2026-03-19. Column cost_per_minute_cents NUMERIC(12,6) on instances table. Usage ticker still runs every 60s but bills exactly 1 minute per tick. Migration 007 renames columns and multiplies existing values by 60.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_credit_idempotency_two_layers = Credit service idempotency has two layers: fast SELECT check outside tx for 99% of replays, plus UNIQUE partial index idx_billing_events_stripe_idempotent on stripe_payment_intent_id catches TOCTOU race where two concurrent webhooks pass the SELECT before either commits. On unique violation the tx rolls back and existing event is returned.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_race_condition_tests = Comprehensive race condition test suite covers 8 scenarios: double entitlement consumption (FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED), double credit (UNIQUE constraint), double entitlement creation (UNIQUE), concurrent provision requests, concurrent balance additions, consume vs refund race, event sequencing (FIFO), concurrent cap increment. All use Promise.allSettled with real concurrent DB operations.

v1.state.security_sprint_complete = Security hardening sprint complete. Round 1 fixed 28 of 31 vulns (0 still open, 2 partial by design). Round 2 fixed remaining 6 new findings: early tier validation in webhook, rate limiter with IP proxy support and memory cleanup, permission downgrade logging in key validator, cap enforcement scope in API response. All routes behind auth middleware except webhook and health.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_tier_pricing = Server-controlled tier pricing in TIER_PRICING: starter $9/mo s-1vcpu-1gb, solo $29/mo s-1vcpu-2gb, team $79/mo s-2vcpu-4gb, enterprise_cloud $199/mo s-4vcpu-8gb. Customer picks tier not price. Checkout sets tier in Stripe metadata server-side.

v1.state.session_end_2026_03_20 = Completed S2 through S5 plus security sprint plus pipeline fixes. Checkout now tier-based not amountCents. Webhook is single atomic transaction. Entitlement model enforces 1 payment 1 instance. Two red-blue audit rounds done. Tests need final green run.

v1.fact.brand_system_fonts = Syne (display/headings 700-800) + Outfit (body 400-500) + JetBrains Mono (data/code). Added via next/font/google with CSS variables --font-syne --font-outfit --font-jetbrains-mono.
v1.fact.logo_design = SVG logomark: outer ring (hash ring) + 4 cardinal blue shard nodes + amber Merkle root center + radial spokes + amber diagonal Markov arc lines. Used in nav and brand guide.
v1.state.next_task_deploy = Next session: deploy updated site to parametric-memory.dev. Check Resend DNS propagation. Set up Google Search Console.
v1.fact.sse_train_animation_queues_sequentially = Multiple train passes on same arc fire as distinct sequential pulses not collapsed. 3 passes = 3 visible bursts. Animation queue stacks events.

v1.procedure.dotenv_file_edit_rule — NEVER modify any dotenv file without first asking the user and clearly explaining what line changes and why. Wait for explicit confirmation. Applies to all env files in all projects.

v1.fact.mmpm_provisioning_state_machine — state flow: provisioning then traefik_pending then running. Provisioner drives provisioning to traefik_pending by polling DO API until droplet active with IP. Traefik-worker drives traefik_pending to running by calling gateway and writing endpoint_url.

v1.fact.mmpm_provisioning_race_condition — DO_READ_TOKEN non-empty causes Express GET instances route to poll DO API on every admin UI refresh. If Express wins before provisioner it sets state running directly bypassing traefik_pending leaving endpoint_url null causing unhealthy. Solution is to keep DO_READ_TOKEN empty in e2e.

v1.fact.mmpm_mock_droplet_unique_ids — mock uses incrementing counter starting at 99001. Each POST /v2/droplets returns a fresh unique ID. Stale droplets from prior runs are inert because provisioner only polls the ID it received from the CREATE response. No mock restart needed between runs.

v1.fact.mmpm_provisioning_destroy_path — DELETE /api/instances/:id sets state destroying, calls deleteDroplet on DO API, calls deregisterSubdomain if traefikApiToken set, sets state destroyed. Mock handles DELETE droplets id and DELETE deregister both returning success.

v1.fact.mmpm_provisioner_error_ambiguity — getDropletStatus in do-client.ts catches ALL errors including 404 and ECONNREFUSED returning null. Both appear as DO API unreachable in logs. Provisioner backoff 5s 10s 20s 40s 60s up to 5 minute timeout.

v1.fact.mmpm_traefik_worker_mechanics — traefik-worker polls traefik_config_queue every 10 seconds using SKIP LOCKED. On gateway success commits queue row completed and instance running in one transaction. On failure resets to pending up to max_attempts then marks failed.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_api_routes = Auth: /request-link, /verify, /logout, /me. Billing: /checkout, /balance, /events, /caps. Keys: POST generate, GET list, /rotate. Instances: GET list, POST provision, DELETE destroy. Webhooks: /stripe payment handler

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_security_layering = Rate limit 100/min global, 10/min billing, 60/min webhooks. Bearer auth->permissions (read/write|read only|none). Session optional. Instance routes require session. Launch gate at app + DB trigger

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_worker_processes = provisioner.ts (droplet + IP poll), traefik-worker.ts (SSL), destroy-worker.ts (snapshot+delete), queue-reaper.ts (timeouts), entitlement-expiry.ts (expiry). Each separate process, own DB pool
v1.fact.compute_managed_pg_17 = Compute uses DO Managed PostgreSQL 17 not Docker PG mmpm-compute-db syd1 basic 15usd

v1.event.daily_briefing_2026_03_22 = Morning briefing generated. Quiet overnight. S16 deploy now 8 days pending. Sprint 1 done, security done. Blockers: deploy site, Resend DNS unverified, Google Search Console not set up, Stripe webhook unconfigured. Sprint 4 tests still pending. Sprint 17 in planning. Suggested focus: deploy, Resend DNS, Sprint 4 tests, Search Console.

v1.fact.knowledge_graph_plan_goal=3D interactive ego-graph knowledge explorer at /knowledge. Public page. Full R3F build. Emergent clusters via force physics. Search to seed, click to expand Markov arcs.
v1.fact.knowledge_graph_tech_stack=React Three Fiber + drei + d3-force-3d + postprocessing. InstancedMesh nodes. LineSegments edges. Same bloom/shader aesthetic as /visualise Substrate Viewer.
v1.procedure.knowledge_graph_sprint3_items=3.1 ArcParticles directional flow. 3.2 CameraControls animate on expand. 3.3 edge weight colour encoding violet-cyan. 3.4 loading and empty states.
v1.procedure.r3f_useframe_explicit_priority=Always set explicit priority on useFrame callbacks when order matters; sim tick at priority -1 so it runs before rendering at priority 0
v1.fact.knowledge_graph_sprint1_rendering_working=Knowledge graph nodes render correctly with type-colored bloom spheres after fixing vertexColors bug and sim double-ticking

v1.event.daily_briefing_2026_03_23 = Morning briefing generated. Quiet overnight. S16 deploy now 9 days pending. Key blockers: deploy site, Resend DNS unverified, Google Search Console not set up, Stripe webhook unconfigured. New item: knowledge graph sprint plan written 2026-03-22, 4 sprints, Sprint 1 not started, needs npm install d3-force-3d. Sprint 4 + 5 tests still pending. Sprint 17 in planning. Suggested focus: deploy, Resend DNS, Sprint 4+5 tests, Search Console.

v1.state.knowledge_graph_sprint_all_phases_complete = All 5 phases of knowledge graph sprint done. Edge storage, bootstrap scoring, MCP removeEdges, seeds+docs+skill, community detection+suggestions. 1383 tests green.

v1.procedure.user_manages_all_git_operations = Do not run any git commands in any folder. The user manages all git operations. Claude runs in a VM and git commands would break the users repo state. User correction from 2026-03-23.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_provision_state_machine = Provisioner Phase 1: create DO droplet (cloud-init: Docker+MMPM image+master+viz keys), INSERT instance=provisioning. Phase 2: poll DO for IP (backoff 5s/10s/20s/40s/60s, 5min timeout), update instance=traefik_pending, INSERT traefik_config_queue row (outbox), mark queue completed. provision_queue: pending→processing→completed|failed.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_destroy_state_machine = Destroy initiated by DELETE /api/instances/:id — marks instance=destroying, inserts destroy_queue row. Destroy worker: Phase 1 snapshot droplet (DO action, 5-20min), Phase 2 poll until completed→INSERT instance_snapshots, Phase 3 DELETE droplet + deregister Traefik SSL→instance=destroyed. destroy_queue: pending→snapshotting→deleting→completed|failed.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_health_check_phase = Missing health check after Traefik registration. traefik-worker marks state=running after SSL registration but never verifies MMPM started. Need new phase: poll GET {endpoint_url}/health with backoff (10s/20s/30s/60s/60s, 5 attempts). Success: state=running confirmed. Failure: traefik_status=health_check_failed, manual intervention.

v1.state.mmpm_compute_sprint_hardening_status — Sprint Droplet Hardening: all 8 tasks (T1-T8) implemented. 13 integration test failures fixed. User needs to run pnpm test:integration locally to verify.

v1.state.mmpm_compute_sprint_hardening_status — Sprint Droplet Hardening COMPLETE. All 8 tasks shipped (T1–T8). 242/242 integration tests passing. Migrations 023, 024, 025 applied to managed DB. GitHub Actions CI gate added. Ready to deploy.

v1.state.mmpm_compute_ci_setup = PR #1 open on trigger-test branch. ci.yml and deploy-compute.yml updated to run full 7-stage preflight. GitHub ruleset active on main: requires PR + Full preflight status check to pass before merge. Block force pushes enabled. User upgraded to GitHub Pro to enable ruleset on private repo. Waiting for CI to pass before merging.

v1.fact.github_branch_protection_method = Using GitHub Rulesets (not classic branch protection rules) on parametric-memory-compute. Ruleset named 'main', Active, targets main branch. Requires pull request (0 approvals) + Full preflight status check. Block force pushes enabled.
v1.fact.github_pro_upgraded = User wjm2202 upgraded to GitHub Pro to enable rulesets on private repo parametric-memory-compute.
v1.procedure.never_delete_files = HARD RULE: Never delete any files. No rm, unlink, or file deletion of any kind.
v1.procedure.never_edit_env_files = HARD RULE: Never edit .env files. Do not read, modify, or suggest edits to any .env file.

v1.fact.finding_sprint_supersedes_chains = Sprint state atoms follow clear chronological ordering that the association agent can detect mechanically. Pattern: knowledge_graph_sprint_status (planning) superseded by sprint_knowledge_graph_phase1_done superseded by kg_sprint_current_phase_5 superseded by kg_sprint_all_phases_complete. These chains now wired.

v1.fact.continuous_semantic_types_vectors = Atoms become positions in continuous space. Type = neighborhood of atom. GNN message-passing propagates information through structure. CDSE uses dual-spectral embeddings. No discrete categories needed—types emerge from learned representations.

v1.procedure.recommend_simple_semantic_kg_approach = Combine (1) semantic embeddings + force-directed physics for visualization, (2) FAISS/HNSW for clustering atoms by cosine similarity, (3) hierarchical k-means on embeddings for emergent ontology, (4) optional: Poincaré embeddings if strict hierarchy needed. Start simple—test if physics simulation + clustering suffices before adding hyperbolic geometry.

v1.fact.semantic_substrate_plan_created = Comprehensive 5-sprint plan for migrating MMPM from discrete atom types (7 fixed bins) to continuous semantic embeddings. Poincare disk for hierarchy, sentence-transformers for vectors, self-organising ontology replaces manual hubs. Plan in mmpm-website/PLAN-SEMANTIC-SUBSTRATE.md

v1.fact.semantic_substrate_tech_stack = Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (22MB ONNX 384-dim) for embeddings. Sarkar construction for Poincare projection. Ward agglomerative clustering for hierarchy discovery. HSL colour mapping from angular position (topic) and radial distance (specificity)

v1.fact.semantic_substrate_edges_unaffected = Knowledge graph edges (6 structural types + Markov arcs) are completely unaffected by semantic substrate migration. Edges reference atoms by key not by type. member_of targets shift from manual hubs to emergent cluster atoms in Sprint 4

v1.event.association_run_2026_03_25 = Association agent run 2026-03-25. Scanned 105 atoms. Found ~95 orphans. Wiring edges across all 7 hubs in batches. Key findings: mmpm_compute_test is a false conflict (complementary infra/structure pair); 3 overlapping git-rule procedures flagged; sprint KG atoms form chronological supersedes chain.

v1.event.sprint0_provenance_implemented_dt_2026_03_25 = Sprint 0 (Domain→Task→Knowledge Provenance) code changes complete. Added domain+task atom types, produced_by edge type, taskContext auto-provenance in session_checkpoint. Typecheck passes.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_exploration_complete = Thorough exploration of compute module within MMPM substrate. Located business plan, architecture decisions, provisioning model, Stripe billing integration details, and technical stack. Compute is separate microservice (mmpm-compute) not in main substrate repo. Runs on memory.kiwi production server via PM2.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_technology_stack = Provisioning: Node.js/Express API + DigitalOcean SDK. Container orchestration: Docker Compose (not K8s). Ingress: Traefik v3 with Let's Encrypt auto-renewal. Storage: named Docker volumes on DO block storage. Monitoring: Prometheus + Grafana per-substrate metrics. Health checks + alerting on container down, memory pressure, proof failures.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_billing_integration = Stripe Checkout with server-controlled pricing. Payment creates billing_event + entitlement (unique on payment_intent_id). Per-minute billing while instance running. Spend caps pause new provisioning. Entitlement expiry worker marks consumed→expired at period_end. Metered billing for overage atoms ($0.001/atom beyond tier).

v1.event.association_run_2026_03_26 = Nightly association agent run 2026-03-26. Scanned ~450 atoms across all types. Phase 1: wired 130+ orphan edges across all 7 hubs. Phase 2: 11 supersedes edges for sprint/test chronological chains. Phase 3: 12 cross-domain bridge edges (viz↔core, compute↔testing). Phase 4: business_plan task active with 48 produced_by edges, no completed tasks. Phase 5: mmpm_compute_test benign conflict, 6 overlapping git-rule procedures flagged. Phase 6: recluster failed (allEdges not iterable server bug).

v1.fact.customer_compose_template_created = integrations/saas/docker-compose.customer.yml: per-customer container pair, isolated bridge network, stop_grace_period 30s, read_only filesystem, resource limits per tier.
v1.fact.tier_resource_limits = free: 256MB heap 384M mem 0.5CPU 2shards 500atoms. indie: 384/512M/0.75/4/10K. pro: 512/768M/1.0/4/100K. team: 768/1G/2.0/8/500K. enterprise: 1024/2G/4.0/8/unlimited.
v1.fact.substrate_host_droplet_ip = mmpm-substrate-syd1-01 IP is 170.64.184.28. 4GB/2vCPU/80GB SSD, SYD1, Ubuntu 24.04 LTS. SSH keys: compute + Mac air. Project: wjm2202. Created 2026-03-26.

v1.procedure.gateway_domain_is_droplet_mcp_nz = Gateway domain is droplet-mcp.nz (NOT mmpm.co.nz). Traefik already running on gateway 134.199.144.251 with DNS-01 via DigitalOcean API. Cert resolver named myresolver. Sidecar API on port 3000. Config dir on host: /root/gateway/config.

v1.fact.gateway_config_repo_is_mmpm_gate = Gateway config lives in mmpm-gate repo (~/Documents/code/mmpm-gate), NOT in mmpm-compute. Contains docker-compose.yml and registertrigger.sh that generates traefik.yml + sidecar + docker-compose.

v1.procedure.cmds_specify_what_where_cmd = When giving commands to user: ALWAYS specify WHAT (purpose), WHERE (which server or local Mac), and CMD (the actual command). User operates multiple servers (compute, gateway, substrate) plus local Mac. Never give bare commands without location context.
v1.fact.deploy_workflow_mcp_health_check = CI/CD deploy-production.yml now checks both /health (data server) and /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server (MCP OAuth discovery) post-deploy.

v1.fact.substrate_tiers_free_indie_pro_team = Four substrate tiers: free ($0, 500 atoms, 100 bootstraps/mo, 50MB), indie ($9, 10K atoms, 1K bootstraps, 500MB), pro ($29, 100K atoms, 10K bootstraps, 2GB), team ($79, 500K atoms, unlimited bootstraps, 10GB). Container resources scale: free=256MB/0.5CPU, indie=512MB/1CPU, pro=1GB/1CPU, team=2GB/2CPU.

v1.event.association_run_2026_03_27 = Nightly association agent run 2026-03-27 (opus consolidation). 1759 active atoms scanned. Phase 1: 340 member_of edges attempted, 250 new edges added, 90 pre-existing skipped. All 7 hubs covered (compute 35 new, testing 48 new, core 42 new, procedures 41 new, sprint_state 39 new, visualization 31 new, corrections 14 new). Phase 2: 17 supersedes edges attempted, 15 new added. Chains: architecture_upgrade 4→5→9→10, KG sprint phases 1→3→4→5→complete, daily briefings chronological, association runs chronological, sprint commerce 2→3, test count 1191→1383. Phase 3: 9 cross-domain bridge references edges, 8 new added. Bridges: test_infra↔false_conflict, test_infra↔website_auth, test_structure↔env_structure, billing↔architecture. Phase 4: No completed tasks found (business_plan still active). Phase 5: 38 conflict groups detected. Known benign: mmpm_compute_test, mmpm_docker_compose. Structural benign: 18 cluster_auto, 19 sci_bench, 8 viz_test. Value-update conflicts: sprint1_progress (3 writes), vscode_mcp_config (2 writes), hardening_status (2 writes). No actionable true conflicts. Phase 6: recluster blocked by allEdges not iterable server bug (same as 03-26 run). TOTALS: 273 new edges committed across all phases.

v1.fact.substrates_table_is_canonical = substrates table (migration 029) is the correct single source of truth going forward. instances table frozen for legacy droplet records only. No new rows written to instances. All new provisioning writes to substrates.

v1.fact.missing_workers_identified = Five workers missing or broken: (1) substrate-provisioner needs rewrite to read substrate_provision_queue, (2) substrate-reaper for substrate queues, (3) grace-period-expiry worker for 30-day cancellation cleanup, (4) substrate workers not in ecosystem.config.cjs, (5) queue-reaper doesnt cover substrate queues
v1.fact.knowledge_viz_edge_cap = GraphEdges.tsx MAX_EDGES=2048 but total edges now 4612 (4529 structural + 83 Markov). Only 2048 rendered. Need to raise MAX_EDGES to ~6000 to show full graph.

v1.fact.compute_ops_high_entropy_transitions = Three highest-entropy intervention points in compute: (1) day 0-7 of grace period (resubscription decision window), (2) window between first balance warning and top-up or suspension, (3) free tier onboarding to first payment decision. These are where substrate intelligence provides most leverage.

v1.fact.compute_ops_markov_feedback = Compute ops Markov feedback loop: every intervention (email/action) creates a feedback atom when outcome is known. memory_train() called on intervention→outcome arc. Over time arc weights encode calibrated model of what works per tier/behaviour cluster. No separate ML system needed — built on MMPM training infrastructure.

v1.fact.compute_dogfooding_pitch = Dogfooding narrative: compute ops run entirely on MMPM substrate. Sales pitch: we don't use rule-based ops management, we use Parametric Memory. Operations AI learned from lifecycle events in knowledge graph. Measurably reduced churn vs threshold-based system. Most credible enterprise demo possible.

v1.fact.compute_ops_cold_start = Compute ops substrate cold start constraint: first 60 days produce no reliable inference (insufficient arc weight history). During cold start fall back to current threshold rules. Substrate accumulates silently. Patterns emerge around day 60-90.

v1.fact.tagline_source_system_learns_from_itself = Marketing tagline source: 'Every new customer improves the system for every other customer.' Network effect framing. Also: 'The observation-intervention loop is self-reinforcing.' The system doesn't just get better with more customers — it gets better with more time, even for a fixed customer base.

v1.fact.saas_plan_ops_substrate_rationale = Strategic rationale for ops memory substrate (SaaS business plan section): The system currently throws away almost all information from state machine transitions. The marginal cost of capturing a signal is near zero (one atom write) but its value compounds because it contributes to every future inference. Information that currently has zero value gets positive and increasing value over time. Customer lifecycle IS a Markov process — MMPM's arc weights converge to actual transition probabilities without explicit statistical work. Every new customer improves the system for every other customer (network effect on operational intelligence). Operations headcount doesn't need to scale with customers. The substrate encodes nuance rules can't express (tier-specific warning timing discovered from data). Strategies become institutional memory not tribal knowledge. The observation-intervention loop is self-reinforcing. Cold start is a built-in A/B test against rule-based system.

v1.fact.saas_plan_ops_substrate_self_hosting = Deepest strategic insight: MMPM is a product whose natural application domain includes managing the product itself. Most products solve the customer's problems. This product solves its own operational problems and in doing so demonstrates its value proposition more convincingly than any pitch deck. The substrate managing compute operations is simultaneously a production system, a learning system, a demonstration system, and a proof of concept — all at once. This is the single most credible enterprise sales narrative available.

v1.state.sprint_ops_substrate_revised = Sprint REVISED: Knowledge-First Operational Intelligence. Design changed from mechanical event bus (23 typed events, rigid atom schemas) to narrative observer pattern. 6 new files, 13 edited files (one observer.observe() call each). Observer gathers context + composes narrative + writes atom + trains arc. Inference agent bootstraps with natural language objectives, produces human-readable digest. First 30 days read-only. Sprint doc at docs/sprint-ops-substrate.md.

v1.fact.provisioning_health_check_spec = Standard health check: GET /health on substrate. Response: 200 {status:healthy, version, atoms:0, uptime_ms}. Poll intervals: shared=every 5s max 12 tries (60s), dedicated=every 5s max 24 tries (2min, more warmup). Non-200 or connection refused = unhealthy, retry. Second check via Traefik URL confirms end-to-end routing.
v1.fact.ops_mcp_brute_force_protection Nginx rate limiting uses map trick: authenticated requests get empty key (skipped by limit_req), unauthenticated get IP-based key throttled to 5r/s.

v1.fact.mmpm_nginx_brute_force_protection = nginx rate limiting for mmpm.co.nz: map checks bearer auth, empty-string key bypasses limit_req. One zone (mmpm_brute_force:10m, 5r/s). Applied to all endpoints. Direct API (/) also rejects 401 at nginx. MCP/OAuth only rate-limited. Health probes exempt. Template rendered via envsubst at deploy time.

v1.fact.knowledge_scientific_viz_three_paradigms = Scientific /knowledge viz uses three layered paradigms: (1) Topographic Landscape — Poincare positions as 2D ground plane, Gaussian KDE for elevation, atom type colors terrain. (2) Temporal Stratigraphy — Z-depth = log(age), translucent strata bands at week/month/quarter, time scrubber for excavation. (3) Markov Flow Field — vector field from weighted sum of arc directions, instanced arrow particles advecting along probability streamlines.

v1.fact.m1d_0_context_failure_summary = Main agent burned weekly budget on self-inflicted iteration during the M-1D sub-zero session because it began editing the sidecar entrypoint without fully reading the surrounding gateway compose file. Three specific patterns emerged: guessing an environment variable name that was plainly visible on the interpolation line, probing the service on a host port that the same file declared as exposed-only, and missing a pre-existing runtime package-manager install antipattern in the service command. Root cause was writing code before finishing context.

v1.fact.app_root_rate_limit_ip_scoped_src_human = The Express application root rateLimit at src slash app.ts near line 128 is keyed by client IP, initialised before session middleware, and defaults to 100 requests per minute. Under supertest, every request presents as 127.0.0.1, so large integration suites saturate this bucket. Sprint 2 added a globalRouteRateLimit option to AppDeps so suites can raise the ceiling without touching production defaults. Applied in substrates-routes, idor-suite both app instances, legacy-shims, cap-api, and billing-status-slug. The substrates-rate-limit suite keeps production defaults because it pins the actual limit behaviour.

v1.event.sm20_landed_dt_2026_04_12 = SM-20 cap-exceeded refund path landed. Migration 066 expands substrate_subscriptions.status CHECK to include cap_exceeded. New helper src/services/cap-refund.ts (refundAndCancelForCapExceeded) with 5 outcome paths. Webhook marks status atomically in INSERT ($6 param binding). Post-COMMIT try/catch calls helper + emits substrate_cap_refund. Idempotency keys: cap_refund:<sub_id> and cap_cancel:<sub_id>. Trial path: skip refund AND cancel, trial expires naturally. 17 unit test assertions (10 helper + 7 webhook wiring) + 4 DB-backed schema assertions + narrative coverage additions. tsc + vitest 162/162 green.

v1.procedure.refund_idempotency_key_pattern = Stripe refund + cancel use separate idempotency keys scoped to subscription id: cap_refund:<sub_id> and cap_cancel:<sub_id>. Separate keys so a retry where refund succeeded but cancel failed can re-fire cancel without re-firing refund. Stripe dedupes idempotency keys within 24h.

v1.procedure.composer_migration_preserve_router_side_concerns — When migrating auth from router-level middleware into a per-route AuthComposer, audit every router-level concern that depended on req.session being populated (rate-limit keyFn, logging, audit-log stamping) and explicitly preserve those dependencies via a separate router-level mount. Composer owns AUTH ENFORCEMENT; it does NOT own session attachment timing for side concerns.

v1.procedure.read_package_json_before_suggesting_pnpm_cmds — Never suggest a pnpm script name from memory. Read package.json scripts field first. mmpm-compute separates test/test:integration/test:ops/test:security/test:swagger/test:db by config file; guessing at pnpm vitest run <file> ignores include/exclude and runs the wrong config or no tests.

v1.procedure.express5_router_prototype_monkey_patch_for_mount_prefix — Express 5 dropped layer.regexp and layer.matchers[0] is a closure — no escape hatch to recover a sub-router's mount prefix from the layer alone. Solution used in route-policy-drift.test.ts: monkey-patch Router.prototype.use ONCE in beforeAll to stamp each sub-router handler with a __routePolicyDriftMountPrefix symbol-key when registered via app.use(prefix, subRouter). Patch is additive (calls original use), idempotent (patched=true guard), and shared-memory-safe across the integration worker — other test files see the harmless tag, behaviour unchanged. Walker then recurses layer.handle.stack joining prefixes via trailing-slash-safe joinPath. Canonical pattern for any future Express 5 route introspection test.

v1.procedure.mmpm_website_e2e_prod_safety_rules = E2E suite against parametric-memory.dev MUST be read-only: never click submit on login/signup, never click confirm-upgrade-confirm, never click admin-rotate-key, never click pricing CTAs that lead to Stripe checkout. Open+close-only patterns for change-plan sheet. Mutating tests must be prefixed mutating_ and excluded from default run.

Pre-registration is enforced by scripts/check-testids.mjs (source must be in registry, registry MAY have unwired entries). Designed explicitly for Playwright + AI-agent automation.

v1.fact.legal_posture_standard_saas = MMPM legal posture is Standard SaaS: broad B2B discretion (suspend, throttle, change pricing, indemnification, force majeure) but consistently preserves mandatory consumer-protection rights (NZ Consumer Guarantees Act, EU UTD, UK Consumer Rights Act 2015, Australian Consumer Law, CCPA). Every aggressive clause includes the carveout 'Mandatory consumer protection rights in your jurisdiction are unaffected'. This is the deliberate trade-off chosen 2026-04-26 to maximise enforceability across consumer courts.

v1.fact.compose_project_name_mismatch_silent_fail_src_human = docker compose down without -p uses the directory basename as project name. If running containers were started with COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME or -p set to a different value, down silently exits 0 having done nothing. Always pass -p <project> matching the com.docker.compose.project label on the running container. Discovered 2026-04-26 cleaning the substrate host.

v1.procedure.sql_no_guessed_params = User correction 2026-04-27. Never write SQL (or any DB command) with guessed parameter values that look authoritative. Specifically: do NOT conflate ops-substrate atom keys (e.g. acct_<hex8>) with actual DB primary keys (UUIDs in accounts.id) — they are different naming conventions. If a real ID/UUID/slug is needed and not in hand, either (a) ask the user to paste it, (b) use a clearly-marked placeholder like <REPLACE_WITH_ACCOUNT_UUID>, or (c) omit the SQL entirely and describe the query shape in prose. The user runs all SQL themselves (Ground Rule 4), so a fake-looking literal becomes a footgun the moment they copy-paste it.

v1.fact.atom_spec_v1_canonical_key_plus_inline_body = An atom has two parts: canonical key (before ` = `) used for lookup and conflict detection, and optional inline body (after ` = `) where the knowledge lives. We store facts and knowledge, not data — the canonical key alone is a slot, the body holds the claim.

v1.procedure.atom_lookup_uses_canonical_key_only = When using memory_access, edges, taskContext, or conflict detection, refer to atoms by canonical key (the part before ` = `). The inline body is preserved by storage and returned by search but ignored by lookup and conflict detection.

v1.procedure.descriptive_hub_form_is_canonical = For the canonical 8 hubs, the description-suffixed form is canonical (`v1.other.hub_mmpm_core = Hub: core MMPM server...`). The bare form is retired: edges rewired to the descriptive form, then bare form tombstoned. Reason: hubs are knowledge atoms describing what belongs in their cluster, not just labels.

Phase 3 dual-membership for cross-domain atoms. Phase 4 routine task reinforcement skipped (no clear produced_by edges to query). Phase 5 reconfirmed known false conflicts (mmpm_compute_test, mmpm_docker_compose). Phase 6 skipped — re-clustering not triggered.

v1.fact.finding_memory_access_no_edge_payload_2026_05_02 = During 2026-05-02 nightly run, memory_access with depth:1 returned currentData/currentProof/predictedNext but no explicit edges array, making mechanical orphan detection unreliable. Workaround: add member_of edges idempotently for high-confidence candidates from semantic search; let server dedupe. Flagged as a tooling improvement target — surfacing edge list in memory_access response would unblock future orphan audits.

v1.procedure.db_changes_via_sql_migration_files_only_2026_05_03 = HARD RULE (user correction 2026-05-03 mid-S1.2): all DB changes — including local-dev data fixes — go through .sql migration files in the migrations/ directory, drafted with the migration skill. NEVER paste raw SQL into pgAdmin to fix data. The migration file gets run via pnpm db:migrate (or equivalent) by the user. Migrations must be idempotent so the same file is safe to apply on dev, managed, and prod even when the broken row only exists on one of them — typically achieved via a WHERE clause that filters to mismatched rows, so the UPDATE affects 0 rows where there's nothing to fix. Read-only SELECT queries in pgAdmin are fine; INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/ALTER/DROP/TRUNCATE are not. Counter to my prior Action 1 in this session which proposed a raw UPDATE — that approach is wrong, replace with a migration file.

v1.procedure.interactive_diagnostics_one_cmd_at_a_time = During interactive debugging, give exactly ONE command per turn. Each command states WHAT (action), WHERE (which machine), WHEN (now or sequenced), and whether it has side effects. User pastes the result, then I respond with the next single command. NEVER batch multiple probes or queries into a for-loop or numbered list during debugging — if the first command fails or surprises us, the rest are stale and waste tokens. Multi-step plans are for execution after the cause is known, not for diagnosis.

v1.fact.merkle_kernel_fromleaf_factory = MerkleKernel.fromLeafHashes(hexHashes) is the spec-conformant path for building a kernel whose leaves are already-computed 32-byte hashes (e.g. master tree over shard roots, per SPEC-SNAPSHOT.md §6.4 Check B sub-check 4). Decoded hex bytes go directly into the kernel as Merkle leaves with NO further hashing. Distinct from the constructor (which SHA-256-hashes its raw string inputs and is correct for atom-key-as-leaf trees). Mixing the two silently produces incompatible Merkle roots — this was the root cause of the masterRoot mismatch fixed in sprint S-AC-29.

v1.fact.spec_snapshot_2_6_aligned_with_6_4 = SPEC-SNAPSHOT.md §2.6 (master tree descriptive prose) was reconciled with §6.4 Check B sub-check 4 (binding verifier obligation) on 2026-05-09. Old text said "padded to next power of two with 32 zero bytes" — inconsistent with MerkleKernel.rootOfHashes (RFC 6962 strict, duplicate-last on odd). New text: shard roots decoded from hex to raw 32-byte buffers used DIRECTLY as Merkle leaves; pair-hashing follows §2.3 (RFC 6962 strict). Domain separation (CT 0x00/0x01 prefixes) explicitly deferred to a future v1.x — v1.0.0-rc matches Bitcoin precedent. Sources: web research on Bitcoin txid leaves, Git tree object IDs, RFC 6962 (CT).

v1.fact.snapshot_v1_envelope_canonical_shape = SnapshotV1 canonical shape (mm-memory/markov-merkle-memory/src/snapshot/types.ts): formatVersion='1.0.0' at top level; tree.* NESTED (masterRoot, treeVersion, treeHeadTimestampMs, shardCount, shardRoots[], shardLeafCounts[], atomCount, edgesRoot, auditLogRoot?); exporter.{name, version, host, exportedAtMs, exportedAtIso}; atoms[]/edges[]/hubAtoms[] top-level; signature.{alg='Ed25519', kid, publicKey, publicKeyFingerprint, keyUri, sig}. NO signature.value, NO signature.keyId, NO signedPayloadSha256 — signing target is canonicalize(snapshot with signature.sig="").

v1.fact.5_customer_substrates_audit_canonical_identity_complete_dt_2026_05_11 = All 5 existing pre-S-AC customer substrates (hidden-edge, keystone-moss, rugged-depot, sunlit-mirror, verdant-lagoon) now satisfy AI Act Article 26(6) traceability: signed snapshots embed .exporter.host=<slug>.droplet-mcp.nz (regulator-citable, slug-stamped) rather than the Docker container hash. Closed gap was 8 audit env vars (7 Vault + MMPM_EXPORTER_HOST) missing from docker-compose.yml on 4 of 5 substrates, plus MMPM_EXPORTER_HOST missing from all 5 .env files. .env backfill + sed-based compose patch + force-recreate. No regression on the 7 Vault keys (signing was already working).

v1.state.sprint_spec_extraction_pending_decisions = SPRINT-SPEC-PACKAGE-EXTRACTION work paused 2026-05-12 pending user decisions on three open questions: (1) S-PKG-01 package home — user leaning toward mmpm-cli/packages/spec/ as third option (not in original sprint doc which offered mm-memory monorepo vs sibling repo); (2) inside mmpm-cli, whether to move existing CLI to packages/cli/ for clean monorepo or keep flat at root; (3) dev-link strategy mm-memory uses to consume the spec — file: link, cross-repo workspaces, or publish-and-pin. S-PKG-02 (npm scope) is decided: PUBLIC @parametric-memory/spec. Claude's recommendation: develop in mmpm-cli/packages/spec/ with full monorepo (packages/cli/ + packages/spec/), hybrid dev path = file:link during S-PKG-04 through S-PKG-07 then publish 0.1.0-alpha.0 then swap mm-memory to ^0.1.0-alpha then promote to 0.1.0 by S-PKG-08. Rationale: later-split is cheap via git subtree split if the three split-triggers appear (external contributor, ownership split, release cadence divergence); separate-repo overhead unjustified for ~1400 lines until evidence demands it. Key facts surfaced: mmpm-cli has 5 src files + 5 tests, mid-build (verify.js+help.js referenced but missing); mm-memory's spec surface is 32 importing files (18 tests, 14 runtime); merkle.ts is wire-format footgun with byte-for-byte parity as hard guardrail #5. Sprint doc at markov-merkle-memory/docs/sprints/SPRINT-SPEC-PACKAGE-EXTRACTION.md.

v1.fact.ngram_hash_embedding_256dim_fnv1a_char_trigrams: NgramHashEmbedding uses FNV-1a hashing of character 3/4/5-grams into 256-dim Float32Array vectors with L2 normalization. Zero external dependencies.

v1.fact.ngram_embeddings_catch_morphological_not_semantic: Character n-gram embeddings capture morphological similarity (typecheck↔typechecking) but cannot bridge true semantic gaps (web↔fastify). Model2Vec needed for semantic.

v1.procedure.ask_user_to_install_packages_when_pip_npm_blocked_src_human: When pip or npm installs are blocked by sandbox proxy, ask the user to install the package. They can do installs that Claude cannot.

v1.fact.sse_proxy_connection_leak = SSE proxy route.ts line 32 has no AbortSignal on upstream fetch. When browser disconnects the proxy fetch continues indefinitely. Every page refresh creates phantom connection. 44 ghosts from ~20 refreshes across 2-3 devices

v1.fact.visualiser_sequential_staggered_growth = Atoms under animation grow to 2x via windowed bell-curve per atom based on sequence position. Each atom grows and returns to normal in order creating ripple effect.

v1.event.sse_animation_pipeline_confirmed_working_dt_2026_03_18 = SSE animations confirmed end-to-end. session_checkpoint triggers commit events (add/tombstone/train cascades). memory_access triggers access events (amber pulse). memory_search and memory_session_bootstrap are read-only and emit no SSE events. User verified live in browser.

v1.fact.sse_proxy_works_but_fragile = Next.js App Router SSE proxy at /api/memory/events pipes upstream ReadableStream to browser. Works but fragile — App Router not designed for long-lived streams. Architecture review recommends direct browser-to-server SSE with JWT token exchange.
v1.fact.compute_access_control_design = Customer access uses prefixed identifiers with 128bit CSPRNG and SHA256 hash only DB storage and show once pattern and invalid access degrades to read only
v1.fact.compute_ticker_gap_recovery = Ticker writes last_tick_at per instance on restart calculates gap and bills retroactively

v1.procedure.memory_access_requires_full_atom = memory_access tool requires the full atom string including the value suffix after equals sign not just the v1 type name prefix use memory_search for lookup by name only

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_credit_idempotency = Credit service is idempotent on stripe_payment_intent_id. Second call with same ID returns existing event without modifying balance. Prevents double-credit from webhook replay.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_launch_gate_architecture = Launch gate has three layers: app-level canLaunch pre-check, provision_queue for process isolation from DO, and PostgreSQL BEFORE INSERT trigger on instances enforcing balance and cap and active status with row lock

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_entitlement_model = Billing changed to 1-payment-1-instance model. Each Stripe payment creates one entitlement (UNIQUE on payment_intent_id). Entitlement consumed atomically by DB trigger on instance INSERT. Max droplet liability equals number of confirmed payments. No balance pool or credits accumulation for provisioning.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_entitlement_trigger = Launch gate trigger now checks entitlements table not balance. Uses FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED to find available entitlement, atomically sets status to consumed with instance_id. No entitlement means no instance even with raw SQL.

Security headers applied.

v1.procedure.db_schema_managed_by_init_sql = All DB schema changes are managed exclusively via init.sql. No DROP TABLE statements in init.sql — only CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS. Migration files exist only for the integration test rollback cycle, never for production schema management.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_architecture_complete_analysis = Full architecture exploration completed: all TypeScript source files read, 22 SQL migrations analyzed, services/workers/API routes documented, Stripe webhook flow mapped, provisioning/destroy queues understood, database schema fully mapped, Traefik gateway integration detailed, state machines documented

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_instance_state_machine = States: provisioning->traefik_pending (IP assigned)->running (SSL done)|paused (enforcer)->destroying->destroyed. Cost tracked per second during running. Snapshots captured on user destroy. Launch gate trigger blocks: inactive account, balance insufficient, spend cap hit

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_queue_system = provision_queue (pending->processing->completed|failed|rejected). traefik_config_queue (pending->processing->completed|failed, outbox pattern). destroy_queue (pending->snapshotting->deleting->completed|failed). queue-reaper resets stale rows every 5min: provision timeout 15min, destroy timeout 45min

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_provisioning_workflow = provisioner.ts Phase 1: create DO droplet via cloud-init, insert instance=provisioning, Phase 2: poll DO for IP (5min timeout, exponential backoff), update state=traefik_pending, insert traefik_config_queue row (outbox)

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_traefik_gateway_flow = traefik-worker.ts reads traefik_config_queue, calls Gateway API POST /register with subdomain and IP, on success: instance state=running. On destroy: DELETE /deregister removes config

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_destroy_workflow = destroy-worker.ts Phase 1: snapshot droplet, Phase 2: poll completion, insert instance_snapshots, Phase 3: delete droplet and deregister Traefik. Snapshots 5-20min (poll 15s). 3 retries. Final: state=destroyed
v1.state.knowledge_graph_sprint_status=Sprint plan written 2026-03-22. 4 sprints defined. Sprint 1 not started. npm install d3-force-3d required (user to run). Page route /knowledge public no auth.

v1.procedure.knowledge_graph_sprint1_items=1.1 page scaffold + middleware whitelist. 1.2 knowledge-store.ts Zustand. 1.3 knowledge-api.ts fetch utils. 1.4 useForceGraph hook d3-force-3d. 1.5 KnowledgeScene R3F canvas.

v1.procedure.knowledge_graph_sprint2_items=2.1 SearchBar ego seed. 2.2 GraphNodes InstancedMesh. 2.3 GraphEdges LineSegments effectiveWeight opacity. 2.4 click-to-expand neighbours. 2.5 drei Text hover labels. 2.6 side panel atom detail.

v1.fact.knowledge_graph_critical_risks=middleware auth catch-all may block /knowledge (must whitelist). weights endpoint may not be in ROUTE_MAP proxy (check DYNAMIC_PREFIXES). force sim must tick in useFrame not setInterval. new nodes must seed near clicked node not origin.

v1.fact.mmpm_bootstrap_edge_scoring = Bootstrap scoring: connectivity boost = min(1 + 0.05 x sum_confidence, 1.25). Supersedes penalty = 0.95^n per incoming supersedes edge. Both multiply together. Result-set subgraph attached as edges field in BootstrapMemoryItem.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_sprint_hardening_tasks = Sprint tasks: T1 phantom droplet fix (tag-based Phase 1 idempotency), T2 mock fault injection (FAULT_CREATE_RATE, TIMEOUT_DROPLET_SUFFIX, RATE_LIMIT_AFTER, control endpoints), T3 DO client 429 backoff + 404 vs 500 distinction on delete, T4 MMPM health check phase after Traefik, T5 snapshot fail should not silently delete, T6 per-account instance cap DB trigger, T7 7-scenario integration test suite, T8 deploy keys claim flow, T9 DO runbook.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_instance_cap_trigger = Missing DB-level instance cap: per-tier hard limits on active instances (not destroying/destroyed). Tier caps: starter=1, solo=2, team=5, enterprise_cloud=20. Enforce as BEFORE INSERT trigger on instances. Defense against attacker with many legitimate entitlements from stolen cards.

v1.procedure.cowork_vm_package_installs_blocked — VM has no npm registry access (403 Forbidden). node_modules are macOS-installed (darwin-arm64 native binaries). Package installs and pnpm commands must be run by the user on their own machine, not by Claude in the VM.

v1.fact.instance_cap_before_launch_gate_ordering — enforce_instance_cap is BEFORE INSERT (migration 023); enforce_launch_gate is AFTER INSERT (migration 011). BEFORE fires first so second provisioning INSERT for starter accounts gets INSTANCE_CAP not LAUNCH_GATE. Tests updated to accept either.

v1.procedure.never_run_pnpm_shell_cmds = CRITICAL: Never run pnpm commands or shell commands directly. Always ask the user to run them. Never modify files to run tests. Never modify .env files except to add missing values. Never delete files without asking first with exact reason and command.
v1.other.hub_mmpm_core = Hub: core MMPM server, Merkle tree, 4-shard architecture, PostgreSQL schema, SSE events, API, auth, Markov chains
v1.other.hub_memory_procedures = Hub: MMPM memory system procedures — checkpoint, tombstone, train, edges, session workflow, atom naming rules
v1.other.hub_corrections = Hub: user corrections to Claude behaviour — hard rules, team protocols, terminal command rules, git rules

v1.fact.finding_corrections_hierarchy = Correction procedures form a natural parent-child hierarchy. v1.procedure.we_work_as_a_team is the parent (general team protocol). user_manages_all_git_operations, never_commit_on_behalf, always_specify_cmd_context are specific rules that constrain it. constrains edge type correctly captures this. Query hub_corrections to surface all rules at once.

v1.fact.hyperbolic_embeddings_poincare_hierarchy = Poincaré ball model naturally encodes hierarchical structures with exponential volume growth. DeER and HyperGatE achieve better KG completion. GGBall (2025) combines VAE + Riemannian flow for graph generation. Reduces distortion in hierarchies vs Euclidean. Works well for ~1000 atoms.

v1.fact.semantic_embeddings_for_knowledge_graphs = Model2Vec distills sentence transformers to small fast models (8-50MB). on2vec uses GNNs + transformers for ontology embeddings. Self-organizing graph reasoning converges to critical state. Embeddings cluster semantically related atoms automatically.

v1.fact.graph_community_detection_embedding_space = Node2vec embeds communities as separable clusters. CDSE and spectral methods identify hierarchy. GCNs with clustering on latent space. Greedy algorithms initialized with embeddings. Emergent community structure from learned representations.

v1.fact.poincare_disk_scientific_basis = Hyperbolic space volume grows exponentially with radius (e^(d-1)r) matching knowledge hierarchy branching. General concepts near origin specific concepts near boundary. Trees embed with zero distortion via Sarkar 2011 construction. Distance encodes generality-specificity as geometric property not annotation

v1.fact.produced_by_is_7th_edge_type = produced_by added to EDGE_TYPES as 7th structural edge. Links knowledge atoms to the task that created them. Immutable after creation. MCP schema updated with enum and description

v1.fact.mcp_remote_auth_cache_path = ~/.mcp-auth/mcp-remote-<version>/ stores OAuth client_info, code_verifier, and lockfiles per server hash. Stale files here cause auth loops after server redeploy. Fix: rm -rf ~/.mcp-auth/mcp-remote-*/ then restart Claude Desktop
v1.event.mcp_oauth_flow_verified_dt_2026_03_25 = Full OAuth flow verified working via curl. All four endpoints respond correctly through nginx reverse proxy to mmpm-mcp:3001
v1.fact.substrate_dockerfile_hardened_with_tini = Dockerfile updated: added tini init for signal forwarding and zombie reaping. ENTRYPOINT tini, CMD node.
v1.fact.substrate_routes_created = src/api/substrate/routes.ts: POST provision, DELETE deprovision, GET status, GET fleet. Internal service auth with timing-safe comparison.

v1.fact.migration_027_adds_substrate_columns = migration 027: adds tier, container_project, subdomain, mcp_endpoint, max_atoms, heap_size_mb, data_path columns to instances. Creates destroy_queue table. Expands state enum.

v1.state.sprint1_s1_s2_code_written_dt_2026_03_26 = S1.1 (Dockerfile hardening) and S1.2 (substrate provisioner + destroyer + routes + migration) code written. Needs typecheck, integration testing, Traefik static config.

v1.procedure.traefik_is_separate_gateway_droplet = Traefik runs on its own gateway droplet (ubuntu-s-1vcpu-1gb-syd1-01-gate, 1GB/1vCPU/25GB, SYD1, Ubuntu 24.04). NOT on the compute droplet. Traefik config changes must be pushed to gateway, not written locally on compute.

v1.fact.infrastructure_three_tier_architecture = Three separate components: (1) compute droplet 2GB/2vCPU/90GB for Express API + workers, (2) gateway droplet 1GB/1vCPU/25GB for Traefik, (3) managed PostgreSQL. All in SYD1.

v1.procedure.careful_droplet_hardening = User has been locked out of droplets before during hardening. Always verify SSH access works BEFORE applying firewall rules or disabling root. Test in stages, keep a backdoor open until confirmed.

v1.fact.gateway_traefik_config_location = Gateway Traefik config lives in mmpm-compute repo at gateway/ dir. Deploy to /opt/mmpm/traefik/ on gateway droplet (134.199.144.251). Dynamic configs at /opt/mmpm/traefik/dynamic/ written by substrate-provisioner via SSH.
v1.fact.compute_config_base_domain_mmpm_co_nz = config.ts baseDomain default changed from droplet-mcp.nz to mmpm.co.nz. substrate-provisioner already used mmpm.co.nz via its own BASE_DOMAIN env var.

v1.event.s1_3_gateway_sidecar_verified_dt_2026_03_26 = Gateway sidecar fully verified: /health, /register, /deregister all working through Traefik TLS at api.droplet-mcp.nz. Sidecar uses expose+Docker labels (NOT ports mapping to host). 4 active routes confirmed.

v1.fact.substrate_state_machine = Substrate provisioning: provision_queue pending->processing->completed, instances provisioning->active. Destroy: instances active->destroying->destroyed, destroy_queue pending->processing->completed. Single-phase, no snapshots. Traefik config via SSH not queue.

v1.fact.substrate_billing_model_subscription_based = Substrate billing uses Stripe subscriptions (not one-time payments like compute). One subscription = one substrate, billed monthly. Free tier provisioned at signup without charge. Paid tiers go through Stripe Checkout. Cancellation triggers 30-day read-only grace period then deprovision. Overages metered at sub-cent per atom above tier limit via Stripe usage records.

v1.fact.mm_website_auth_magic_link = mm-website uses magic link auth via Resend email. Middleware protects /admin and /dashboard routes. Auth proxy forwards session to mmpm-compute. New /dashboard page shows substrate usage and upgrade/cancel.

v1.fact.mm_website_nextjs15_react19_tailwind4 = mm-website stack: Next.js 15.2.0, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4.0, TypeScript 5.7, App Router with server components. Dark theme. Zustand for state. New substrate-tiers.ts config added for billing display.

v1.fact.compute_two_disconnected_provisioning_models = init.sql + migrations 001-026 built droplet model (instances table, entitlements, one-time payments). Migration 029 created separate substrates table with own provision queue and subscription billing. substrate-provisioner.ts writes to instances table but webhook writes to substrates table — completely disconnected. Neither pipeline is complete end-to-end.

v1.fact.launch_gate_trigger_blocks_substrates = enforce_launch_gate trigger on instances table requires entitlement with matching droplet_size. Substrates use subscriptions not entitlements and have no droplet_size. Trigger would block every substrate INSERT. Solution: substrates table is separate so trigger doesnt fire.

v1.fact.provisioner_testing_strategy = Three-layer testing: L1 unit (state machine, tier limits, vitest threads), L2 integration (Testcontainers PG + mock-do-server + Stripe test-mode HMAC webhooks + mocked SSH, 29 isolated DBs), L3 property (fast-check random event sequences against state machine). All run in CI, zero production infrastructure.

v1.fact.checkout_mode_mismatch = Compute /api/checkout creates Stripe mode:'payment' (one-time) sessions. Substrate model needs mode:'subscription' sessions so Stripe fires subscription.created webhooks that substrate-stripe.ts handles. Current checkout works for legacy droplet model only.

v1.state.pricing_page_purchase_enabled = Purchase buttons re-enabled on pricing page 2026-03-27. PricingCTA.tsx rewritten with live Stripe checkout flow. Beta callout removed. FAQ updated. JSON-LD availability changed from PreOrder to InStock. Enterprise-cloud tier ID mismatch flagged but not yet fixed.

v1.fact.compute_ops_substrate_design = Compute ops substrate design: ops-writer service hooks into 6 state machine transitions (provisioner, substrate-provisioner, destroy-worker, grace-period, entitlement-expiry, billing-enforcer), writes non-PII operational atoms per account (hashed ID), inference agent bootstraps substrate to decide intervention timing/content. Feedback loop trains Markov arcs from outcomes. Phase 1: instrumentation only. Phase 2: feedback atoms. Phase 3: inference agent as recommender. Phase 4: autonomous.

v1.fact.tagline_source_rules_replaced_by_knowledge = Marketing tagline source: 'We don't use rule-based operations management. We use Parametric Memory.' Contrast angle: traditional SaaS runs on hardcoded thresholds, cron jobs, and human ops staff. MMPM-powered SaaS runs on learned knowledge that improves itself. No ops headcount scaling needed.

v1.fact.compute_ops_scope_billing_help = CORRECTION: compute ops substrate is NOT just email notifications. Full scope includes: billing assistance (help users understand charges, manage spend caps, understand tier options, troubleshoot payment), lifecycle management (provisioning, suspension, reactivation, destruction), proactive billing guidance (infer when user needs help before they ask), spend optimisation recommendations, tier upgrade/downgrade guidance. The substrate encodes billing intelligence, not just notification timing.

v1.fact.saas_plan_billing_intelligence_scope = The ops substrate is NOT just notification optimisation. Full scope: billing assistance (help users understand charges), spend guidance (cap recommendations), lifecycle management (provisioning through destruction), tier recommendations (usage-driven upgrade suggestions), proactive intervention (infer when user needs help before they ask), churn prevention (pattern-matched early warning), and onboarding optimisation (activation email timing from first-session behaviour). The notification service is one consumer. The inference agent, dashboard, and future AI-assisted support are others.

v1.fact.ops_substrate_narrative_not_events = DESIGN CORRECTION: Ops substrate atoms must be narrative observations, not structured event records. Each atom reads like a case note written by a knowledgeable operator: includes the immediate signal PLUS full account context (tier, customer age, recent events, usage levels, warning history, intervention outcomes). Atoms are queryable against any future question because they contain semantic richness, not predetermined fields. No event bus, no typed channels — one observer service that gathers context from Postgres and composes natural language atoms.
v1.state.sprint_ops_substrate_implemented: Sprint ops-substrate implemented. 6 new files, 13 edited files. tsc --noEmit passes zero errors. 2026-03-27.

v1.procedure.ops_observer_inject_pattern: Observer injected as optional dep to service factories. Workers create opsObserver = createOpsObserver(pool) at top. All calls are fire-and-forget void — never awaited by callers.

v1.fact.ops_observer_health_endpoint = GET /health/observer returns observer pipeline stats: enabled, circuitState, bufferSize, totalObserved, totalWritten, totalDropped, totalPiiBlocked, totalTrainFailed. Returns 200 when enabled+closed, 503 when degraded. Added in app.ts after main /health endpoint.

v1.fact.provisioning_snapshot_strategy_approved = DO golden image snapshot strategy approved for dedicated tier provisioning. Create golden droplet: Ubuntu 22.04 + Docker + pre-pulled mmpm-service + mmpm-mcp images. Snapshot it. Use snapshot_id as image field in POST /v2/droplets. Cuts provisioning from 5-7min to ~90-120s. Update golden snapshot on each new release by creating temp droplet from current snapshot, pulling new images, snapshotting again.

v1.fact.cowork_web_search_blocked_bug = Cowork MITM egress proxy blocks all non-API domains regardless of allowlist UI settings. Confirmed March 2026 bug. GitHub issues #30861 #38984 #30112. Allowlist config pipeline broken: UI entries never applied to proxy. Workaround: Settings → Network Access → Allow all domains. Infrastructure fine, just config pipeline broken.

v1.fact.traefik_atomic_switch_pattern = Atomic Traefik route switch: (1) write NEW conf file while old still exists - two routes briefly (ok, both healthy), (2) reload Traefik, (3) verify new route /health via Traefik, (4) only then delete OLD conf file, (5) reload again. Rollback: if step 3 fails, delete new conf - old route never removed. Never a zero-route window.

v1.fact.free_tier_now_1_dollar_stripe_checkout = Architecture change 2026-03-29: free tier subscription uses $1 Stripe charge at checkout. Previous direct-enqueue path removed. All 5 tiers go through identical Stripe Checkout flow. Webhook subscription.created fires for every tier including free.
v1.fact.ops_mcp_http_transport ops-mcp converted from stdio to Streamable HTTP transport. Runs as Docker container on port 3002. Same architecture as mm-memory mmpm-mcp.
v1.fact.ops_mcp_unified_auth Single auth key used at all three layers: nginx map directive, ops-mcp checkAuth, and substrate. Separate auth key removed.
v1.fact.ops_mcp_no_direct_http ops-api location removed from host nginx. Only ops-mcp path exposed externally. All substrate access goes through MCP tools only.
v1.fact.ops_mcp_deploy_checklist docs/ops-mcp-deploy-checklist.md has full production deploy steps and verification commands.
v1.procedure.ops_mcp_cwd_not_supported_by_claude_desktop Claude Desktop MCP config does NOT honour cwd field. Always use absolute paths in args.

v1.event.mcp_connection_fix_dt_2026_03_30 = Fixed MCP 401 loop: mcp-remote OAuth flow completed but server rejected the bearer. Diagnosis via docker logs showed bearer mismatch between client config and server env. Resolution: updated claude_desktop_config.json to match server, removed stale local session files, restarted Claude.
v1.fact.compute_env_production_path = Production env file: /srv/parametric-memory-compute/.env.production (symlinked from .env). App dir: /srv/parametric-memory-compute

v1.fact.knowledge_scientific_viz_architecture = ScientificView is parallel scene graph alongside existing KnowledgeSpaceDrift. Components: SemanticTerrain (PlaneGeometry height-mapped from Poincare KDE), TemporalStrata (translucent time planes), MarkovFlowField (instanced cones + particle advection), TerrainAtoms (pinned to surface), TerrainContours (cluster boundary lines). Shares knowledge-store.ts data. ViewToggle extended to 3 modes: semantic, provenance, scientific.

v1.procedure.billing_portal_return_url_manual = BILLING_PORTAL_RETURN_URL env var must be set manually in production .env: BILLING_PORTAL_RETURN_URL=https://parametric-memory.dev/dashboard. Claude cannot patch .env files. The compute app uses deps.baseUrl ?? localhost:3000 for the portal returnUrl — so baseUrl env var must also be set to https://parametric-memory.dev in production.

v1.fact.substrate_auth_sync_locations = Substrate auth is written to 3 places at provision time: (1) substrates row in PostgreSQL as a digest, (2) the config file on the substrate host via SSH, (3) the nginx conf on the substrate host as a literal string used for rate-limiting. Rotation requires updating all 3 then restarting containers.
v1.state.key_rotation_sprint_status = Sprint planned 2026-04-04. Not started. KR-1 backend 3h, KR-2 wiring 30m, KR-3 frontend stepper 2.5h, KR-4 modal 30m, KR-5 test 1h, KR-6 docs 20m.

v1.fact.seo_meta_strategy = SEO targets: 'AI agent memory', 'MCP memory server', 'persistent memory for AI agents', 'Claude memory persistent'. JSON-LD SoftwareApplication schema on home page. Sitemap priority: home 1.0, pricing 0.9, about 0.8, docs 0.7, blog 0.6.

v1.state.launch_review_2026_04_05 = Launch review April 5 2026. 20 days to target (April 25). Backend launch-ready. 9 open security findings from round 3 — all MEDIUM or LOW. Pre-launch checklist gates 1-5 are infrastructure tasks not yet done. Fix RD-01 (auth/verify rate limit) and RD-08 (webhook response leaks IDs) before launch. Website repo not mounted — separate repo from compute.

v1.fact.compute_droplet_env_production_path = .env.production lives at /srv/parametric-memory-compute/.env.production — this is COMPUTE_DEPLOY_PATH from GitHub Actions. PM2 runs from this folder. Edit with: nano /srv/parametric-memory-compute/.env.production

v1.fact.instagram_marketing_architecture = Instagram marketing system lives at mmpm-compute/instagram-marketing-system/. Three skills: instagram-ad-creator (visual content generation), instagram-poster (Graph API publishing), instagram-marketing-coordinator (strategy/calendar). Three scheduled tasks: weekly-instagram-content-planning (Sun 6pm), daily-instagram-content-creation (weekdays 7am), daily-instagram-post (weekdays 9am). All NZT.

v1.state.instagram_week_2026_W15_plan = Week of 2026-04-06. 5 posts planned: Mon=pain_point/infographic/broad_dev (stateless AI frustration), Tue=technical_deep_dive/code_snippet/ml_developer (3 lines of code), Wed=product_feature/diagram/enterprise_cto (audit trails), Thu=thought_leadership/quote_graphic/founder (memory as moat), Fri=educational/video/broad_dev (60s explainer). Inaugural coordinated week, no prior performance data. All 4 segments covered. 2 infographic-equivalent, 1 code_snippet, 1 diagram, 1 quote_graphic, 1 video.

v1.state.website_capacity_event_driven = Capacity feature refactored from ISR-polling (60s) to event-driven. PricingCardClient.tsx wraps each tier card with client-side capacity state. Fresh check fires on CTA click via POST /api/capacity/check. Badge shows 'Available' by default (fail-open), updates after check. Waitlist form appears dynamically if tier is full. Old ISR fetch removed from pricing page.tsx server component.

v1.fact.capacity_compute_side_todo = Compute-side work still needed: (1) after provisioning completes in substrate-provisioner.ts, trigger capacity cache invalidation so next check sees updated slots. (2) Optionally add cache-bust param to GET /api/v1/capacity for on-demand fresh reads bypassing 60s in-memory cache.

v1.state.compute_arch_audit_findings = CRITICAL: port allocation race (substrate-provisioner SELECT MAX without lock), unbounded Maps in billing-enforcer. HIGH: SSH command interpolation in tier-change-worker, StrictHostKeyChecking=no. MEDIUM: single-instance rate limiting, no jitter on polling workers.

v1.state.compute_hardening_sprint_plan = 6 sprints planned: (1) port allocation race fix via pg sequence, (2) unbounded Map TTL eviction in billing-enforcer, (3) SSH command safety with shell-safe validation + SCP refactor, (4) BoundedMap utility, (5) SSH host key hardening with centralized ssh-config, (6) polling jitter across all 11 workers. Order: 1 first, 2-3-4 parallel, 5 after 3, 6 last.

v1.fact.destroy_instance_sudo_gated = Instance destruction (DELETE /api/instances/:id) is sudo-gated when TOTP enrolled. Pattern: check totpService.isEnrolled first, then requireSudoToken only if enrolled. requireSudoToken throws SudoTokenInvalidError (not NoTotpEnrolledError) when token missing — enrollment check happens at issuance not consumption. Non-TOTP users fall through to typed confirmation only.

Recent atoms wired: TOTP hardening event, process lifecycle event, capacity sprint event, sudo/billing facts, instagram task+state, website SEO/billing states, landing page atoms, git_claude_never_commits procedure, nightly run records. v1.event.compute_stale_pm2_after_deploy_dt_2026_04_08 = Deploy #35 (PR #30 merge) ran successfully on GitHub Actions but compute-server PM2 process at memory.kiwi is still running old code. All /api/v1/* routes return 'Cannot GET' while older /api/* routes work. Root cause: pm2 reload with tsx did not pick up new code. Deploy completed in 31s (vs normal 4-8min) suggesting pnpm install may have been a no-op but pm2 reload failed silently.

v1.fact.compute_deploy_uses_pm2_reload = Compute deploy (deploy-compute.yml) SSHs to memory.kiwi, does git reset --hard origin/main, pnpm install --frozen-lockfile, db:migrate:prod, then pm2 reload ecosystem.config.cjs --env production. PM2 runs tsx directly (no build step). pm2 reload may not invalidate tsx module cache — consider switching to pm2 restart for reliability.
v1.fact.pricing_no_public_free_tier = No public free tier. Pricing page $9/$29/$79 is correct. Free tier ($3 internal cost) is internal/beta only.

v1.fact.mmpm_needs_stats_endpoint = MMPM service does not expose usage metrics. Needs new GET /stats endpoint returning atomCount, bootstrapCount (per period), storageBytesUsed. This blocks live usage metering, dashboard accuracy, and Stripe overage billing.

v1.fact.m0a_root_cause_full_chain = Bug M-0A full causal chain: (1) GitHub push triggers deploy, (2) server-deploy.sh runs pm2 restart --only compute-server which is a HARD restart not zero-downtime, (3) port 3100 briefly has nothing listening, (4) nginx proxies during gap gets ECONNREFUSED and generates default HTML 502 page because no proxy_intercept_errors or error_page directives existed, (5) website proxy forwarded raw HTML to browser, (6) pricing page JSON.parse crashed. Two-layer fix applied 2026-04-09: nginx now returns JSON for 502/503/504 via proxy_intercept_errors + named locations, and compute-proxy.ts validates all upstream responses are JSON.

v1.fact.m0a_deploy_causes_downtime = Every deploy to mmpm-compute causes a brief outage window because PM2 hard-restarts Express (SIGTERM → wait 5s → start new). During the restart gap port 3100 is unresponsive. PM2 also restarts 13 workers sequentially after main server. No graceful handoff exists — potential future milestone M-0C.

v1.fact.nginx_json_error_pages_api = nginx memory.kiwi.conf.template now has separate location /api/ block with proxy_intercept_errors on and error_page directives for 502/503/504 pointing to named locations @api_502 @api_503 @api_504 that return JSON with application/json content type. Non-API catch-all location / left without interception for webhooks/redirects.

v1.event.m0a_complete_dt_2026_04_09 = Milestone M-0A (Fix Capacity Proxy Endpoint) completed. Two-layer fix: (1) nginx location /api/ with proxy_intercept_errors and JSON error pages for 502/503/504, (2) shared computeProxy utility across all 15 website proxy routes. Preflight passes. Root cause documented: PM2 hard restart during deploy leaves port 3100 briefly dead, nginx served default HTML.

v1.fact.orchestrator_active_atom_counter = ShardedOrchestrator maintains _activeAtomCount field. Initialized once in init() via listAtoms().filter(active). Incremented in addAtoms(), decremented in removeAtom(). Exposed via get activeAtomCount(): number. O(1) reads for metrics.

v1.fact.milestone_program_doc_location = Parametric-Memory-Milestones-Program.docx in mmpm-website root. v2.0 Evidence-Based, created April 9 2026. 6 phases, 17 milestones + 3 pre-launch. 18.5-28 estimated sessions. Critical path: P1A stats endpoint -> P2A-C observability -> P5B E2E tests.

v1.fact.m1d_data_migration_not_started_src_research = M-1D Substrate Data Migration confirmed NOT started as of April 10 2026. tier-change-service.ts line 273 comment says Future LevelDB rsync migration between hosts. Current handleSharedToDedicated is fresh-start only. Pro to Team upgrade loses all atoms and edges. Blocker for selling Team tier with data continuity guarantees. Depends on M-1B finish.

v1.procedure.smoke_test_sidecar_via_prod_https = To exercise the Traefik sidecar: ssh gateway, load the authz env var from the gateway dotenv file into a local shell variable, then POST JSON to https://api.droplet-mcp.nz/register. NEVER curl localhost:3000 — docker-compose uses expose: not ports:, so the host cannot reach port 3000.

v1.fact.gateway_sidecar_expose_only = mmpm-gate docker-compose.yml uses expose rather than ports for port 3000. Port 3000 is only on the internal Docker network (sidecar and traefik containers). Host localhost:3000 is unreachable. ALL sidecar smoke tests must go through https://api.droplet-mcp.nz which Traefik routes to sidecar:3000.
