v1.fact.rd01_verify_rate_limit_already_shipped_src_code = RD-01 (GET /api/auth/verify dedicated rate limit) was already fixed in src/api/auth/routes.ts lines 87-89 of parametric-memory-compute before 2026-04-11. The fix applies rateLimit({windowMs: 60000, max: 5}) to the /verify route with a SEC-RD01 comment. Discovered during the 2026-04-11 pre-launch security sweep when Claude verified the finding against current code and found it already remediated. The 2026-04-05 launch review state atom saying this was a must-fix is stale.

v1.event.rd08_webhook_id_leak_fix_dt_2026_04_11 = On 2026-04-11 Claude edited src/api/webhooks/stripe.ts in parametric-memory-compute to strip internal UUIDs and the pre-assigned subdomain from four res.json response bodies: the charge.refunded success branch, the idempotent fast-path branch, the happy-path checkout.session.completed branch, and the UNIQUE-violation race branch. Each edit moved the IDs into a structured console.info log line so operators retain observability. Each touched block carries a SEC-RD08 comment. New regression tests were added to tests/integration/stripe-webhook.test.ts inside a describe block named SEC-RD08 that uses a scanForLeaks helper with UUID and Stripe-prefix regex plus a BANNED_KEYS set to assert the response bodies match exact shapes. Awaiting the user to run npm run preflight on the Mac to validate.

v1.event.rd08_preflight_green_dt_2026_04_11 = On 2026-04-11 the RD-08 webhook ID leak fix passed the full preflight suite on the user's Mac. All six preflight stages (typecheck, unit, db, integration, property, security) reported green. The 4 new SEC-RD08 regression tests in tests/integration/stripe-webhook.test.ts passed alongside the 5 pre-existing stripe-webhook tests. RD-08 is now closed as a launch blocker. Combined with the earlier discovery that RD-01 was already shipped, both 2026-04-05 must-fix security findings are resolved. Code is not yet committed — user runs git per team protocol.

v1.state.sprint_c_equals_m1d_substrate_data_migration = Sprint C (~12h, provisioner) from memory maps to Sprint 1.4 / M-1D Substrate Data Migration (Cross-Host) in the Milestone Program doc. NOT a separate ADR. User confirmed 2026-04-11. Goal: build substrate-migration-service.ts with 7-state pipeline (source_read_only → backing_up → transferring → restoring → verifying → cutting_over → complete), wire into tier-change-service.ts replacing 'fresh start' paths, add backup-before-destroy safety net to substrate-destroyer, new substrate_migrations table. Blocks M-5B E2E per-tier journey tests. Quote: 'the most important infrastructure sprint in the program.'

v1.fact.m1b_remaining_crash_recovery_bugs_src_research = M-1B (Dedicated Droplet → Substrate Chain) is ~90% shipped via unified substrate-provisioner.ts from Sprint A/B rewrite. Two crash-recovery bugs remain (~0.5 sessions): (1) processPendingQueue recovery branch at substrate-provisioner.ts:1027-1064 does not select hosting_model/droplet_ip/host_id, so a crash mid-dedicated-provision recovers against the wrong (shared) host; (2) Phase 0 queue-only crashes where droplet was created but substrate row not yet inserted fall through the JOIN-based query and sit in 'processing' forever — needs a separate branch that reads queue.phase='droplet_creating' + droplet_id and either resumes from container_deploy or marks failed. NOT blocking M-1D but should ship alongside.

v1.fact.website_dashboard_vs_admin_roles = /dashboard = read-only glance grid showing ALL substrates (active + decommissioned), with live health/SSL/tier/https badges on active ones only, status-only display for decommissioned. No management actions anywhere. /admin = administrative list of all substrates (grouped or filtered active vs decommissioned). Both dashboard cards and admin list rows navigate to /admin/[slug] on click. /admin/[slug] = detail page with all management actions (usage, rotate key, upgrade tier, cancel, reactivate, deprovision, MCP server block).

v1.fact.website_decommissioned_detail_policy = /admin/[slug] for a decommissioned substrate (status = deprovisioned | destroyed | provision_failed): read-only review (final usage snapshot, provisioned/decommissioned timestamps, tier at decommission, MCP endpoint greyed/non-copy, billing events). ONLY action exposed = Reactivate button, visible ONLY when status=deprovisioned AND grace_period_ends_at > now AND Stripe subscription still reactivatable. No data export. No hard-purge button.

v1.fact.website_legacy_proxy_cutover = Full one-PR cutover. Delete Next.js proxies: /api/my-substrate/*, /api/compute/[...path], /api/substrate/[...path], /api/checkout. Replace with new proxies under /api/substrates/* and /api/billing/* that forward to /api/v1/substrates/* and /api/v1/billing/* on compute. Compute still exposes legacy shims for other clients, but mmpm-website no longer uses them after this PR.

v1.fact.website_multi_substrate_scope = Build list UI correctly for N substrates per account, but optimize empty-state and copy for 0-or-1 (current reality: all accounts have 0 or 1). No filter chips, no search, no pagination until a real customer actually has 2+. Status grouping on /admin (active first, decommissioned below) is enough sorting for v1.

v1.fact.compute_new_substrates_contract_prefix = New compute multi-substrate contract lives at /api/v1/substrates/*. Session-authenticated. Slug-scoped. Rate limited 60/60s per account. v1.event.sm15_accounts_has_used_trial_migration_064_landed_2026_04_12 = SM-15 landed 2026-04-12 as migration 064_accounts-has-used-trial.sql in parametric-memory-compute. v1.procedure.cap_exceeded_atomic_insert_not_update = When marking a subscription as cap_exceeded, stamp the status in the original INSERT INTO substrate_subscriptions (as the $6 parameter binding) rather than a separate UPDATE. This makes the cap_exceeded state atomic with the row creation — if the post-COMMIT refund fails, the row already reflects the correct state without a race window.

v1.fact.refund_id_passes_pii_scanner = Stripe re_ prefixed refund IDs (e.g. re_abc123) are allowed by the ops narrative PII scanner. Only cus_ sub_ and pi_ prefixes trigger PII detection. This means refundId can safely appear in narrative text without sanitization.

v1.fact.multi_substrate_api_design = Compute has slug-scoped endpoints at /api/v1/substrates/:slug/* for all substrate ops (cancel, reactivate, rotate, claim, deprovision, usage, rotation-status). List endpoint GET /api/v1/substrates returns {substrates:[]} sorted by status priority. All return 404 substrate_not_found for non-owned slugs (no existence leak). Rate limit 60req/min/account. v1.fact.mmpm_website_session_model = Opaque 256-bit hex token, SHA-256 hashed in compute auth_sessions table, sent to compute as Authorization: Bearer, cookie name mmpm_session (HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax; Max-Age=30d). Auth-method-agnostic — compute only sees accountId after lookup.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_owns_accounts_and_sessions = Compute is source of truth for accounts and sessions, not the website. Website has no users DB. accounts.email is UNIQUE (001_create-accounts.sql). magic_link_tokens and auth_sessions tables in 022_create-auth-tables.sql. verifyMagicLink() creates accounts on-the-fly at auth-service.ts:180-189.

v1.fact.mmpm_website_auth_proxy_files = Auth proxy at src/app/api/auth/[...path]/route.ts, compute proxy at src/app/api/compute/[...path]/route.ts, magic-link callback at src/app/auth/callback/route.ts. All forward Authorization: Bearer from mmpm_session cookie. Middleware at src/middleware.ts gates /admin and /dashboard on cookie presence only — no role check there.

v1.fact.mcp_oauth_provider_is_mcp_server_not_user_login = mmpm_oauth_provider.ts (compute side) is the OAuth 2.1 server for MCP clients, not an OAuth client for user login. Different concerns. Do NOT confuse with Google/GitHub login work. Will share no code with ADR-003 OAuth login feature.

v1.fact.adr_003_alternatives_rejected = Rejected Auth.js (replaces working magic-link code path, wrong data ownership), Lucia (v3 deprecated 2024), hardcoded no-interface (abstraction pays off at second provider), generic OIDC framework (GitHub is not OIDC), website-side identities table (creates second source of truth for accounts).

v1.fact.oauth_pkce_state_store_design = Server-side PKCE/state/nonce store keyed by state. Single entry holds provider, verifier, nonce, returnTo, createdAt, used flag. TTL 5 minutes (stricter of state vs PKCE recommendations wins since they share the entry). consume() is atomic read+mark-used+return, returns null on expired or reused. Background sweep every 60s.

v1.fact.compute_substrate_state_machine_verified_dt_2026_04_14 = Verified 2026-04-14 from migration 029_substrate-subscriptions.sql. substrates.status CHECK: provisioning → running → read_only → suspended → deprovisioned. substrate_provision_queue.status: pending → processing → completed | failed. substrate_subscriptions.status: active → past_due → cancelled | paused. Destroy handled by substrate-destroyer worker (soft destroy retains data 30 days, hard destroy is GDPR). Grace period worker polls substrates where status=read_only AND grace_period_ends_at passed, transitions to deprovisioned.

v1.fact.finding_memory_access_cross_shard_404_dt_2026_04_20 = memory_access tool returns 404 when the queried atom lives in a shard other than the one handling the request. Atoms exist and are retrievable via memory_search (which iterates shards) but single-shard POST /access fails with not-found-in-shard. Impact on consolidation agent: Phase 1 orphan-state verification via edgeBoost is unreliable; mitigation is to rely on append-only idempotent session_checkpoint semantics (duplicate member_of edges get skipped server-side, which this run confirmed — 8 of 63 edges skipped).

v1.fact.oauth_bridge_route_requires_rawbody_capture = OAuth bridge routes must be mounted with express.json({ verify: captureRawBody }) from compute src/middleware/bridge-auth.ts before the bridgeAuth middleware. Without rawBody capture the middleware returns 500 with a configuration error. Session 1 task 1 (mount routes in app.ts) must include this wiring. Re-serialising JSON for hashing is unsafe — numeric precision and key order can differ from what website signed.

v1.fact.oauth_provider_evidence_wire_format — cross-repo wire contract: providerEvidence is a discriminated union with two variants tagged by `kind`. Google variant carries an OIDC id-assertion string; GitHub variant carries an OAuth user-authorisation string. Added to NormalizedClaims (website, src/lib/auth/providers/types.ts), forwarded in /bridge/signin and /bridge/link POST bodies (src/lib/auth/oauth-callback.ts), required in VerifiedClaimsBody (compute src/api/docs/features/oauth.ts — ProviderEvidenceSchema as z.discriminatedUnion). The other legacy fields (email, emailVerified, providerSub, displayName) are now ADVISORY — the authoritative answer comes from re-verifying the evidence on compute.

v1.fact.mmpm_website_docker_compose_uses_default_env_file = docker-compose.yml on web droplet has no env_file directive and deploy.yml uses no --env-file flag, so docker compose reads the default .env file (NOT .env.prod) from /home/deploy/parametric-memory-web/. As of April 25 2026 the .env file on the droplet has all six required OAuth env keys configured.

v1.procedure.compute_oauth_diagnostic_chain_dt_2026_04_25 = OAuth bridge 404 diagnostic chain for parametric-memory-compute: (1) Confirm /login HTML has signin-google testid via curl (rule out website-side bake). (2) curl POST /api/v1/auth/oauth/bridge/signin on prod compute. 404 HTML 'Cannot POST' = route not mounted. JSON {error: 'Not found'} = mounted but enabled=false. 401 = working. (3) Check process env: cat /proc/<pid>/environ. (4) Check pm2 picks up env_production: must use `pm2 restart ecosystem.config.cjs --env production --update-env` not bare restart. (5) Check for rogue root processes: ps -ef | grep ^root | grep -E 'tsx|node'. Non-tini parents = orphan pm2 daemon. (6) Probe other routes (/api/auth/me, /api/v1/signup) to confirm app.ts is executing — if signup mounts but oauth doesnt, the route is being served by a different (stale) process holding the port.

v1.procedure.legal_pages_edit_workflow = When editing legal pages: (1) AskUserQuestion to confirm posture (Standard SaaS / Max / Min), new-section scope, location. (2) Backup files before edit. (3) Use Python with anchored string replacements + assertions, not regex on whole file. (4) Add data-testid to each new clause. (5) Register testids in docs/DUAL-ACCESSIBILITY.md BEFORE running guard:testids. (6) Add invariant test in src/app/__tests__/ that reads source files and asserts key clauses + testids present. (7) Run npm run lint && typecheck && guard:all && test:run. (8) Hand user the cleanup .bak rm command and the commit message — never commit/rm yourself.

v1.fact.b4_root_cause_starter_missing_from_container_limits = B4 (admin shows Free-tier limits for Starter customers) root cause: src/workers/{mock-tier-change-worker.ts:39-47, substrate-provisioner.ts:176, tier-change-worker.ts:94} all use the pattern `CONTAINER_LIMITS[tier] ?? CONTAINER_LIMITS.free` but the CONTAINER_LIMITS map has free/indie/pro/team/enterprise but NO starter entry. When a substrate's tier is set to starter, the workers silently fall back to free defaults (500/100/50) and write those to the substrates row's denormalised max_atoms/max_bootstraps_month/max_storage_mb columns. The /api/v1/my-substrate route reads those columns directly. Fix: add starter entry {maxAtoms:1000, maxBootstrapsPerMonth:200, maxStorageMB:100} to all three workers + unit test + 1-row UPDATE in dev for quiet-summit. Prod has 0 affected rows because no customer has bought Starter yet.

v1.procedure.no_advertise_free_or_one_dollar_plans_2026_05_03 = HARD RULE (user correction 2026-05-03): MMPM never advertises a free tier or a $1 plan to users on any user-facing surface. The Basic ($1/mo internal) tier exists ONLY as the cold-storage fallback per D7 — it is `publiclySold: false` in tiers.ts and must NEVER appear in pricing pages, FAQ answers, JSON-LD product offers, llms.txt summaries, actions.json catalogues, or any other surface a prospect or customer could see. The lowest advertised tier is Starter at $3/mo. Even denial copy like 'there is no free plan' is borderline and should be removed in S1.1 trial cleanup unless it serves a clear customer-confusion-prevention purpose. CI guard added to S0.3 scope (check-pricing-content.mjs) must fail on any user-facing surface containing the strings 'free tier', 'free plan', '$1 plan', '$1/mo', or '$1 per month' (the publiclySold:false tiers.ts row is allowed because it's not a user surface).

v1.procedure.never_commit_with_failing_tests_2026_05_03 = HARD RULE (user correction 2026-05-03 during S1.2 commit prep): never commit when there are failing tests in the working tree, even if the failure is from another sprint's in-flight work. The 'this isn't our test' framing is wrong — if it's failing in our working tree, we own fixing it before we commit. Root-cause the failure first; only commit once preflight is green. The earlier suggestion to ship S1.2 with the unrelated stats-key test failure (or to use git stash to hide it) violated this rule and should not be repeated. Apply: any time preflight is red, the next action is diagnosis + fix, not workaround.

v1.fact.starter_3_dollars_razor_thin_margin_2026_05_03 = S0.4 unit-economics spike finding (2026-05-03): Starter at $3/mo is razor-thin (≤5% gross margin) even with all viable optimisations applied (Lever 1: CPU overcommit 0.5→0.1 vCPU per shared substrate; Lever 3: per-substrate Postgres eliminated — substrate uses LevelDB only). Lever 2 (multi-tenant single process) is OFF the table per mm-memory architecture review — substrate is self-contained-by-design (Docker compose up; one customer per process; no tenant abstraction in src/). Realistic per-Starter cost ≈ $3.00-3.05 vs price $3.00. To achieve 40% gross margin (matching Solo/Pro/Team safety profile), Starter price must rise to $5/mo. Other tiers OK at canonical pricing: Solo $9 (56%), Pro $29 (79%), Team $79 (45%). Spreadsheet evidence: audit/tier-economics-2026-05-03.xlsx (Recommendations sheet, sensitivity analysis Starter $3 vs $5).

v1.fact.substrate_provisioner_recovery_robust_dt_2026_05_04 = substrate-provisioner.ts crash-recovery is robust and complete as of 2026-05-04. Code at lines 1163-1325 has: (a) RecoveryRow SELECT pulls spq.hosting_model/droplet_id/droplet_ip + LEFT JOIN compute_hosts for ch.ssh_target/ip_address — dedicated recovery uses the correct host SSH target; (b) explicit Phase 0 (Branch A) recovery for queue-only crashes — verifies droplet via getDropletStatus, resets to pending if alive or marks failed if gone; (c) Phase 1-4 (Branch B) for substrate-row-exists crashes; (d) NEW 2026-05-04: Branch B explicit fall-through handler that marks rows failed with descriptive error_message when slug/port/substrate_id are not all set (previously silent no-op); (e) phantom droplet detection via findDropletByTag; (f) orphan reconciler runs every 36 cycles. The earlier April-2026 atoms describing crash-recovery gaps were superseded by Sprint M-1B completion.

v1.procedure.mirror_canonical_types_dont_redeclare_optionals = When porting a shared type (e.g. SnapshotV1) into a downstream package, mirror exactly: same field names, same required-ness, same nesting. NEVER redeclare with everything-optional — that compiles silently against the wrong shape and is exactly how mmpm-cli got the legacy flat-shape parser past code review for months. Add a source-of-truth comment naming the canonical file. Add a fixture-based test that loads a real golden file from the canonical repo (skip-if-missing so CI without the checkout still passes) — that fixture is the spec-drift regression guard.

v1.state.cli_snapshot_check_canonical_v1 = mmpm-cli/src/checks/snapshot.ts on canonical SnapshotV1 shape as of 2026-05-11. Surface check only: shape + formatVersion + signature envelope. Deep crypto (Merkle reconstruction from shardRoots, edgesRoot recomputation, JWKS resolution, Ed25519 verification) still TODO — port from mm-memory/markov-merkle-memory/scripts/check-substrate-readiness.ts (A6–A16 assertions).

v1.procedure.audit_3_layer_env_compose_container_before_substrate_ops = Before any compose-touching ops on customer substrates, run a 3-column audit per slug: .env (file present + key=value line), docker-compose.yml (environment block forwards the key as ${KEY:-}), running container (docker exec test -n "$KEY" returns 0). Reveals asymmetries — e.g. compose patched without recreate; .env value present but compose missing pass-through; partial earlier runs. Cheap, read-only, prevents acting on stale assumptions and prevents redundant work. Pattern proven on 5-substrate S-AC rollout 2026-05-11 where keystone-moss showed compose-patched-but-not-recreated state that simple act-on-assumption would have missed.

v1.procedure.bash_sed_with_tempfile_and_rollback_for_compose_yaml_patches_over_pyyaml = For production customer docker-compose.yml patches prefer bash + sed -i "/anchor/r tempfile" with .bak.<ns> backup, post-insert grep verification of all expected keys, and automatic restore on partial insert — over PyYAML safe_dump which reformats whitespace and re-quotes values even on no-op runs. sed preserves the original file byte-for-byte except for the inserted block. PyYAML patcher (patch-compose-vault-env.py) is fine for green-field provisioning and for tests; disruptive on hand-maintained customer files because diff becomes unreadable. Established 2026-05-11 during 5-customer S-AC rollout after user pushback on the patcher approach.

v1.fact.merkle_scene_canvas_setup = MerkleScene.tsx: Canvas camera position [0, 30, 120] fov=55. BG dark #030712. ACES tone mapping exposure=1.2. onPointerMissed deselects. OrbitControls target=[0, 2, 0] autoRotate=0.3 dampingFactor=0.05 min=20 max=300 panSpeed=0.5. Lighting: ambient=0.2, pointLight cyan #7dd3fc at [20,30,20] int=0.4, pointLight magenta #c084fc at [-20,10,-20] int=0.15. Safe wrapper (ErrorBoundary + Suspense) for each R3F child. EffectComposer with Bloom: intensity=0.8 threshold=0.2 smoothing=0.9 mipmapBlur=true

v1.fact.atom_nodes_rendering = AtomNodes.tsx uses InstancedMesh single draw call. sphereGeometry args=[1, 16, 16]. meshBasicMaterial toneMapped=false opacity=0.92 transparent. Per-frame: pre-computes sin/cos, sets matrix + scale via setMatrixAt, colors via setColorAt. selectedIdx/hoveredIdx cached. Floating: sinT*cos(phase)*0.08 Y + cosT*sin(phase*2.33)*0.04 X. Pulse: scale *= 1 + sin(pulsePhase) * (PULSE_SCALE=1.6 - 1). Selected scale *= 1.5, hovered *= 1.25. Type colors boosted 2.2x for bloom. Select color #f0abfc*2.2, hover white*2.2. Clicks via e.instanceId. Hover sets pointer cursor.

v1.fact.store_layout_helpers = memory-store exports: SHARD_DEPTH=8, SHARD_SLOTS=256, RING_RADIUS=5, RING_Y=14, NODE_SPACING=0.8, CONE_RATIO=0.45, ARC_FILL=0.92, LEVEL_DROP=3.5, MAX_VISUAL_DEPTH=7, NUM_SHARDS=4. SHARD_ANGLES: 0->0 (right), 1->π/2 (front), 2->π (left), 3->3π/2 (back). shardRingPosition(sid) returns ring point [cos(angle)*RING_RADIUS, RING_Y, sin(angle)*RING_RADIUS]. treeNodePosition(shardId, depth, posInLevel) computes angular arc distribution: radialAtDepth(d)=5+(2^d-1)*0.45, fraction=(posInLevel+0.5)/nodesAtLevel, naturalSpan=(2^d*0.8)/radial capped at ARC_FILL*(2π/4), nodeAngle=centerAngle+(fraction-0.5)*span. y=RING_Y-1.5-depth*3.5

v1.state.pipeline_fixes_complete = All 7 critical pipeline issues fixed: (1) checkout uses tier-based pricing from TIER_PRICING map not raw amountCents, (2) webhook wraps credit + entitlement in single PG transaction with rollback, (3) dropletSize derived from entitlement not caller, (4) max 3 pending queue items per account, (5) stale queue reaper resets stuck processing items, (6) balance debit uses GREATEST(balance-cost 0) to floor at zero, (7) entitlement expiry worker marks consumed entitlements past period_end as expired and returns instances to pause.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_webhook_single_transaction = Stripe webhook now does balance update + billing event + entitlement creation in a single PostgreSQL transaction. If any step fails all roll back. No more partial state where credits exist but entitlement does not.

v1.fact.mmpm_provisioning_health_check — Express GET /api/instances/:id calls pingInstanceHealth at endpoint_url plus /health with 3s timeout. Health result shown in admin UI. Requires endpoint_url to resolve locally in e2e. Mock instance health server on port 4011 answers GET /health.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_payment_model_entitlements = Stripe Checkout with server-controlled pricing. Webhook creates billing_event plus entitlement with unique constraint on payment ID. Per-minute billing while running. Spend caps pause creation. Entitlement expiry worker marks consumed->expired at period_end
v1.procedure.d3_force_no_restart_with_manual_tick=When ticking d3-force manually in useFrame do NOT call sim.restart(); just set sim.alpha(value) directly; restart causes double-stepping
v1.state.knowledge_graph_sprint1_complete=Sprint 1 core rendering working: 5 seed atoms visible with correct colors bloom and force-directed positions

Phase 4: seeds.json +19 atoms, SKILL.md cloud-updated, CLAUDE.md updated with edge section, parametric-memory.skill packaged.

v1.procedure.mmpm_repo_file_management = Ask before removing any file from the mmpm repo. User manages all git operations — do not run git commands. User standing instructions from this sprint.

v1.event.knowledge_graph_sprint_phase5_complete_dt_2026_03_23 = Phase 5 complete. 5.1: community_detection.ts (pure TS union-find, no graphology needed), getAllEdges() in orchestrator, runCommunityDetection() hooked into POST/DELETE /edges. 5.2: suggestedEdges in bootstrap (coAccessWeight>=5, no structural edge, cap 3). 5.3: communities field in bootstrap. 5.4: 1383/1383 tests passing.

v1.procedure.user_runs_all_npm_commands = When any npm command is needed (install, run, build, test on host), give the command to the user and wait for confirmation. Do not run npm commands directly — the VM sandbox may block registry access or lack permissions. User correction from 2026-03-23.

v1.procedure.mmpm_treeversion_reset_on_restart = treeVersion resets to 1 on container restart — this is expected and confirms hitting the newly restarted container. Data is intact if LevelDB files are present and PPM trie restores correctly. Not a sign of data loss.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_overview = mmpm-compute is the commercial billing/provisioning backend for Parametric Memory. Node.js/TypeScript, Express API + 4 background workers, PostgreSQL DB. Runs on production server memory.kiwi via PM2. Source at parametric-memory-compute/. Stack: Node+tsx, Express, pg, Stripe, Resend.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_pm2_processes = 5 PM2 processes: compute-server (Express API, port 3100), compute-provisioner (DO droplet creation, holds DO write token), compute-traefik (SSL registration outbox worker), compute-destroyer (snapshot+delete droplets, holds DO write token), compute-reaper (resets stuck queue rows every 5min)

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_traefik_state_machine = Traefik worker reads traefik_config_queue. POSTs {token, subdomain, ip} to https://api.droplet-mcp.nz/register. Success: instance.state=running, traefik_status=registered, endpoint_url=https://{subdomain}.droplet-mcp.nz. Failure: retries up to max_attempts(5), then traefik_status=failed. traefik_config_queue: pending→processing→completed|failed.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_billing_model = Tier→droplet: starter(s-1vcpu-1gb $9/mo), solo(s-1vcpu-2gb $18/mo), team(s-2vcpu-4gb $29/mo), enterprise_cloud(s-4vcpu-8gb $79/mo). Stripe webhook single TX: balance+, billing_event, entitlement(status=available), account.tier, reserve subdomain, provision_queue row. 1 payment = 1 entitlement = 1 instance hard invariant.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_billing_enforcer = billing-enforcer runs after usage ticks. Rules: spend cap hit→cap_hit action (no new launches, running instances kept). balance<=0→pause ALL instances. balance<5min burn→urgent_warn. balance<1hr burn→warn. Warnings deduplicated: same type max once per hour per account.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_key_reveal_pattern = master_key_reveal + viz_key_reveal stored plaintext in instances table after provisioning. Cleared atomically on first GET /api/instances/:id. After first read they are NULL forever. Master key = read_write permissions, viz key = read_only permissions on the MMPM instance.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_queue_worker_pattern = All queue workers use FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED on queue tables — safe for multi-replica. Poll interval: 10s for provisioner/traefik/destroyer. Reaper resets stuck rows: provision_queue stuck >10min, traefik_config_queue stuck >10min, destroy snapshotting stuck >45min.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_test_infra = Integration tests: Testcontainers (real PostgreSQL). global-setup.ts starts container, setup.ts creates schema+runs migrations. E2E tests: mock-do-server.js (persistent process, DO API mock on localhost:4010/v2, Traefik mock on :4010/register, health server on :4011). E2E test mode enables /api/auth/dev-magic-link (non-prod only).

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_config_groups = Required env groups: DB connection (DATABASE_URL + pool settings), Stripe integration, Resend email service, BASE_URL for magic links, PORT 3100. Provisioner and destroyer use DO write+read env vars and MMPM_DOCKER_IMAGE. Traefik worker uses TRAEFIK_REGISTER_URL and BASE_DOMAIN. E2E testing has endpoint + mock overrides.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_phantom_droplet_risk = CRITICAL: If Phase 1 POST to DO succeeds but HTTP response is lost, provisioner never records droplet_id. Queue row goes failed, reaper resets to pending, second droplet created. Phantom droplets bill indefinitely. Fix: tag every droplet with provision-queue:{queueId} and query GET /v2/droplets?tag_name=provision-queue:{queueId} before creating to recover existing droplet ID.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_do_api_gaps = DO API gaps: (1) DELETE /v2/droplets/{id} returns 404 if already gone but doRequest throws on 404 indistinguishable from 500. (2) No 429 backoff anywhere - rate limit causes immediate failure. (3) Snapshot match by name is fragile if snapshot still propagating. (4) GET /v2/droplets?tag_name=X not yet used but critical for idempotency.

v1.fact.topic_models_emergent_ontology = hLDA and Pachinko Allocation Model add hierarchies to LDA. Semantic-LDA captures word-concept relationships. HERCULES (2025) combines hierarchical k-means + LLM summarization. TaxoGen constructs taxonomies via embedding clustering. Semi-supervised methods refine structures.

v1.fact.force_directed_graph_for_knowledge_visual = Physics simulation (spring attraction + Coulomb repulsion) converges to equilibrium. d3-force-3d and 3d-force-graph libraries available. Medium graphs (50-500 nodes) show best quality. Symmetry and uniform edge length emerge naturally. Perfect for ~1000 atoms visualization.

v1.fact.mcp_oauth_state_inmemory = OAuthProvider in mmpm_oauth_provider.ts stores all state in JS Maps (zero persistence). Server restart wipes all OAuth state. Clients must re-authenticate from scratch

v1.fact.mcp_config_format_cowork = Cowork MCP config only supports command+args format not url+headers. Use mcp-remote as proxy. Static header via --header flag gets ignored because mcp-remote still does OAuth discovery first

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_provisioning_architecture = Automated isolated substrates model: 1 orchestration droplet → manages N compute droplets. Each compute droplet hosts 30-50 substrate pairs via Docker Compose. Traefik v3 for dynamic routing + auto-TLS per subdomain. Provisioning via Node.js API + DO API. DNS: wildcard *.mmpm.co.nz with {slug}.mmpm.co.nz per customer.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_customer_isolation = Security-first architecture: EVERY customer gets isolated substrate (own container pair, own LevelDB volume, own Merkle tree root). No shared database or multi-tenant substrate. One exploit does not leak other customers' memories. Compliance requirement for healthcare/finance/legal sectors. Scales via automation not consolidation.
v1.fact.substrate_destroyer_created = src/workers/substrate-destroyer.ts: soft destroy (stop containers keep data) and hard destroy (remove data). Cleans Traefik config, updates instance state.

v1.procedure.postgres_is_managed_not_on_droplet = PostgreSQL is a DigitalOcean managed database instance, NOT on the compute droplet. Do not include PG in droplet capacity calculations. Do not plan PG installation on compute droplet.
v1.event.s1_4_substrate_routes_wired_dt_2026_03_26 = Substrate API routes (provision, deprovision, status, fleet) wired into Express app.ts with internalServiceKey auth. Typecheck clean.

v1.fact.gateway_sidecar_architecture = Gateway sidecar is NOT exposed to host network. Uses Docker 'expose' + Traefik labels for internal routing. Traefik proxies api.droplet-mcp.nz -> sidecar:3000 within Docker network. Do NOT use localhost:3000 to test — use https://api.droplet-mcp.nz/

v1.fact.unified_provisioner_architecture = Both models deploy identical thing: mmpm-service + mmpm-mcp Docker container pair. Only difference is WHERE containers run. Shared tiers (free/indie/pro) deploy to existing substrate host. Dedicated tiers (team/enterprise) create DO droplet first then deploy same containers. Phase 1 (acquire host) branches on tier. Phases 2-4 (deploy containers, healthcheck, traefik config) are identical code.

Total ~38.5hrs. Recommendation validated: all 6 issues confirmed still present in codebase. One state machine, one provisioner, one queue.

v1.fact.knowledge_viz_root_cause = mmpm-website ROUTE_MAP in src/lib/mmpm.ts was missing 'edges' and 'poincare' entries. Proxy returned 404 for /api/memory/edges and /api/memory/poincare, so KG structural edges and Poincaré layout never reached the frontend.

v1.fact.knowledge_viz_data_available = MMPM substrate at 2026-03-27: 1464 active atoms, 83 Markov arcs, 4529 structural edges (4294 member_of, 105 produced_by, 46 supersedes, 39 references, 17 depends_on, 16 derived_from, 12 constrains), 55 poincaré coords.

v1.fact.compute_ops_substrate_atom_schema = Ops substrate atom schema: account atoms use hashed ID (one-way, never reversible from substrate), pattern: v1.event.acct_{hash}_{signal}_{date}. Population-level learned patterns stored as v1.fact.pattern.*. Lifecycle state as v1.state.acct_{hash}.lifecycle. Privacy boundary: hash mapping lives only in compute Postgres, never in substrate.

v1.fact.tagline_source_product_is_proof = Marketing tagline source: 'The product IS the proof, and the proof IS the product.' Core narrative: we don't sell a theoretical capability, we sell exactly what we run. When a prospect asks 'does this work?' the answer is 'we run our entire operation on it — billing, lifecycle management, churn prevention, onboarding optimisation.' The demo is your actual ops dashboard powered by your actual substrate trained on your actual data. Competitors can't replicate this without building the same product AND running it on themselves for months. The accumulated arc weight history is a moat.

v1.fact.saas_plan_ops_substrate_moat = Business moat from ops substrate: (1) Accumulated arc weight history represents months of learned intelligence competitors can't copy. (2) Every decision is Merkle-auditable — enterprise compliance teams can trace why any action was taken. (3) The product demos itself — ops dashboard IS the product running on real customer data. (4) Network effects: substrate gets smarter per customer, creating increasing returns to scale. (5) No ops headcount scaling — humans handle only novel cases where no arc has sufficient confidence.

v1.procedure.ops_observer_pattern = Ops observer pattern: one service (ops-observer.ts) called explicitly at each significant moment. Takes accountId + signal type + immediate data. Gathers full account context from Postgres (tier history, recent billing events, usage stats, previous warnings and outcomes). Composes a 2-4 sentence narrative atom. Writes to substrate fire-and-forget. Trains Markov arc from previous atom for same account. No event emitter, no typed bus, no rigid schema.

Not an MCP endpoint issue — the endpoint exists but the internal edge retrieval is broken.

v1.fact.knowledge_scientific_viz_key_distinction = Existing viz is simulation (force physics approximates structure, position emergent). Scientific viz is projection (measured quantities mapped directly to visual channels — Poincare embeddings to position, timestamps to depth, Markov weights to flow vectors). No intermediary physics model. Shows substrate as mathematical object with geometry, dynamics, and history.

v1.fact.telemetry_three_signals = Capacity gating uses three signals: (1) docker stats via SSH per host every 5min — actual container RAM fill, gate at 70%. (2) DO Monitoring API every 30min — host-level mem/cpu/disk, gate mem at 80% disk at 85%. (3) counter-based fill current_tenants/max_tenants gate at 75%. Any signal triggers draining status. Re-open requires counter < 65% AND no resource signal firing.

v1.state.compute_process_guard_refactored = All 10 workers + 2 scripts now use registerShutdown() hook pattern. process-guard.ts has FIFO shutdown hooks, 10s timeout, double-shutdown protection. Tests: 4 passing.

v1.fact.vitest_vm_pool_node22_limitation = Vitest default VM pool + Node 22 triggers ERR_VM_DYNAMIC_IMPORT_CALLBACK_MISSING when any code uses dynamic import() or subprocess spawning. Workaround: use pool forks (see vitest.ops.config.ts) or avoid dynamic imports in tests.

v1.procedure.claude_stale_local_git_warning = NEVER trust local git branch state as ground truth for what is deployed. This sandbox cannot git fetch (SSH blocked). v1.procedure.stats_design_no_sync_io_in_request_path = NEVER put synchronous filesystem walks or full atom scans in HTTP request handlers. Metrics must be side effects of work already happening (maintained counters) or async background tasks. User correction: original design used sync dirSizeBytes + listAtoms().filter() in request path — blocked event loop.

v1.procedure.webhook_swallow_refund_errors_return_200 = The webhook handler wraps refundAndCancelForCapExceeded in try/catch. On failure: log error, emit substrate_cap_refund with outcome=error + errorMessage, still return 200 to Stripe. This prevents infinite webhook retries. The helper itself throws (not swallows) so unit tests can assert failure modes — same pattern as SM-47 observability-side-effects-never-convert-404-into-500.

v1.procedure.no_working_files_in_repos = USER CORRECTION (2026-04-27): Only files that should be deployed to the prod server may live inside any project tree. Working/scratch/safety files (e.g. .bak files made by Claude before in-place edits, scratch markdown notes, temporary diagnostic dumps) MUST NOT be created inside the repo. The fix is NOT to add them to .gitignore — that just hides them. The fix is to NEVER create them inside the repo in the first place. When Claude needs a safety copy before mutating an existing file, write it to /sessions/<id>/mnt/outputs/ (the session scratch dir) or to /tmp, never to a path under any repo mount. When Claude needs to draft markdown/text working notes, those go in outputs/, not at repo root. Repo trees stay tidy: source, configs, deployable assets, and that's it. Bitten in closed-source migration sprint (2026-W18) — Claude left 15 .bak files across mmpm-website (12), parametric-memory-compute (1), markov-merkle-memory (2).

v1.procedure.slug_stability_invariant = Substrate slug invariant (Approach A, 2026-04-27): the slug stored in substrates.slug at INSERT time is what the customer sees in URLs forever. The provisioner does NOT rename. Three call sites use reserveSubdomain at INSERT: signup/routes.ts, checkout/session-route.ts, billing/substrate-checkout.ts. Phase 1 provisioner UPDATE intentionally omits slug from SET clause. Any future code that re-introduces a slug rename must also restore the temp-slug visibility in the multi-substrate dashboard, which is the bug Approach A killed.

v1.procedure.equals_separator_reserved_for_spec_evolution = The ` = ` separator in atom keys is reserved for spec evolution. Keep canonical keys clean so future parsers (e.g. `key =: alias`, `key =@ ref`, `key == typed_body`) can normalise reliably. If a literal `=` must appear inside the canonical key, encode it as `__eq__`.

v1.procedure.verify_atoms_functional_before_proposing_deletion = Before recommending tombstone or deletion of any atom or class of atoms, verify the atom is broken (404 on access AND no search hits AND no edges) — not just non-canonical. Structural divergence from spec is not corruption. Investigate before destruction.

v1.task.atom_v3_spec_review = Review and approve v3 atom spec draft. Decision points: discriminator separator (`#` vs alternatives), metadata field set, conflict-detection algorithm (designated-claim vs hash vs structural), migration cadence (lazy vs scheduled sweep).

v1.fact.tier_change_service_container_limits_distinct_from_substrate_tiers = tier-change-service.ts has its own CONTAINER_LIMITS map (line 105) separate from SUBSTRATE_TIERS in substrate-tier.ts. The worker writes limits.heapSizeMb to substrates.container_memory_mb and parseFloat(limits.cpuLimit) to substrates.container_cpu_cores. Indie worker values: heapSizeMb=384, cpuLimit='0.75' — distinct from SUBSTRATE_TIERS.indie marketing values (containerMemoryMB=512). When asserting post-tier-change DB state, use the worker map values, not the marketing config values.

v1.procedure.python_replace_script_must_save_per_section_or_atomic = When using a Python heredoc to apply multi-section file edits, write each section's changes to disk INSIDE the section (per-file write_text after each modification group) rather than building all changes in memory and writing at the end. If the script crashes after partial work, the in-memory-only changes are silently lost. Cost we paid: a tier-change-service.test.ts script crashed at an assertion mid-section, all changes lost, required full re-run. Pattern: for each file that needs edits, finish the changes for that file and immediately p.write_text(src) before moving to the next file.

v1.procedure.no_unnecessary_env_vars = User correction: when a script's context is single-purpose (e.g. audit:research only ever runs against the research substrate), the value should be hardcoded with a comment, not pulled from an env var. Env vars are configuration surface area requiring docs, defaults, validation, tests. Adding them for hypothetical reuse the user has said will not happen wastes effort and pollutes the codebase. Hardcode by default; introduce an env var only when a real second caller exists or the user asks.

v1.fact.mmpm_client_mode_design = MMPM needs readOnly flag on auth layer. Middleware blocks write and training operations for read-only clients. Side-effect stripping on access and search skips weight updates. Additive change with zero breaking impact on existing operations. Estimated 16-32 hours.

v1.fact.viz_rendering_approach = Particle-based rendering with InstancedMesh for 1-2 draw calls across 5000 atoms. TSL shaders auto-compile to GLSL or WGSL. WebGPU compute handles pulse timing and position physics. UnrealBloomPass for glow. Zustand for state. WebSocket for real-time MMPM events. Bundle approx 300KB gzipped.

v1.state.sprint_v1_step_1_1_design_complete = OAuth2 scope-based approach chosen for client mode. Scope read for read-only clients, scope master for existing. ~65 lines of changes to mmpm_mcp_http.ts. Spec at docs/CLIENT-MODE-SPEC.md. Additive only with zero changes to master path.

v1.fact.merkle_edges_rendering = MerkleEdges.tsx: Static geometry, prefers geometry from worker (store.geometry) fallback to useMemo compute. Two lineSegments groups: treeEdges (parent->child arcs per level) + ringEdges (shard root to ring). localEdges computes parent->2 children for each level recursively. treeEdges color sky-blue #38bdf8*1.2, ringEdges cyan #22d3ee*1.5, both opacity 0.6-0.7 toneMapped=false. Uses bufferGeometry + bufferAttribute for positions. Float32Array format: [x, y, z, x, y, z, ...]

v1.fact.store_selected_hover_pulse = memory-store selectedAtom/hoveredAtom are string|null. selectAtom(atom|null) + hoverAtom(atom|null) actions. pulseAtom(atom) sets pulse=true for 1200ms via setTimeout. Pulse state triggers color lerp toward HOVER_COLOR 0.6 blend. atoms is VisualAtom[] array with key, type, shard, index, hash, resolved, position [x,y,z], pulse bool.

v1.fact.sse_changeset_format = SSE events batched per commit not per atom. Changeset includes added atoms with shard index hash, tombstoned keys, trained arcs, version, rootHash, timestamp. Enables temporal connection visualization.

v1.state.phase_a_pending_cleanup = Once read-only viz confirmed working on production, remove legacy master line from web project dotenv. Only remove after Substrate Viewer loads atoms through read-only proxy.

v1.event.sprint17_planned_two_versions_dt_2026_03_16 = Sprint 17 planned: split mm-memory into clean main (core substrate only) and visualisations branch (SSE, Grafana, harness, load tests). 8 sprint items defined in SPRINT_17.md.

v1.fact.website_401_cause_docker_compose_missing_env = Website 401 Unauthorized caused by docker-compose.yml not passing MMPM connection env vars to container. Fixed by adding both vars. Droplet .env also needs the read-only viz key.

v1.fact.concurrent_test_fix_batched_io = Concurrent stress test fixed by three production code changes: (1) throttle PPM persistence to 30s intervals, (2) batch access metrics into single LevelDB batch write during commit, (3) batch access log entries into same flush. Eliminates 3 individual LevelDB writes per reader access call.

v1.fact.vitest_pool_vmthreads = vitest.config.mjs switched from forks to vmThreads pool. Fork pool doubles Node runtime memory causing OOM on memory-constrained environments with 4-shard concurrent test.

v1.fact.load_test_p95_threshold_13ms = Load test p95 threshold raised from 10ms to 13ms in Sprint 16. Batched I/O throttling adds 2-3ms sequential overhead. Sprint 17 S17-5 targets restoring sub-10ms.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_entitlement_gate = launch_gate DB trigger (AFTER INSERT on instances): checks account.status=active, finds available entitlement with matching droplet_size and period_end>now(), atomically sets entitlement.status=consumed+instance_id. RAISE EXCEPTION rolls back entire TX if no entitlement. FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED prevents race.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_subdomain_system = Subdomains reserved at Stripe webhook time (inside checkout TX). Format: adjective-noun e.g. amber-circuit. Uniqueness enforced by UNIQUE INDEX on instances.subdomain WHERE subdomain IS NOT NULL. Base domain: droplet-mcp.nz. MCP endpoint: https://{subdomain}.droplet-mcp.nz/mcp.

v1.fact.mmpm_compute_instance_key_reveal_flow = Key reveal: provisioner writes plaintext keys into instances table. v1.fact.semantic_knowledge_graph_research_dt_2026_03_24 = Five approaches for self-organizing knowledge graphs: (1) semantic embeddings + vector clustering, (2) hyperbolic embeddings for hierarchies, (3) topic models (LDA/hLDA) for emergent ontologies, (4) continuous semantic types, (5) practical force-directed physics. Simplicity principle: SimplE, TransE, node2vec outperform complex models. GNNs enable message-passing hierarchies. FAISS/HNSW provide vector clustering at scale.

v1.fact.practical_simplicity_knowledge_embeddings = SimplE tensor factorization outperforms complex models. TransE minimal parameters but effective. Node2vec shallow linear networks beat complex approaches. Principle: simpler baselines often match or exceed sophisticated methods.

v1.fact.recommended_stack_semantic_kg = Sentence transformers (all-MiniLM or similar) → embeddings. FAISS for clustering. Force-directed layout for visualization. Hierarchical k-means for taxonomy. GNN message-passing optional. No fixed types needed. Atoms labeled by neighborhood in embedding space.

v1.fact.taskcontext_auto_provenance = session_checkpoint MCP tool accepts optional taskContext param (task atom key). When provided auto-creates produced_by edges from each new atom to the task. Dedup check prevents duplicates

v1.procedure.never_git_commit_from_vm = Claude runs in a VM with mounted folders. Never run git commit, push, or any git write operations. The user handles all git operations themselves on the host machine

v1.procedure.fix_mcp_auth_loop_after_redeploy = When MCP auth loops after server redeploy: 1) rm -rf ~/.mcp-auth/mcp-remote-*/ on the Mac 2) Restart Claude Desktop 3) mcp-remote does fresh OAuth flow (register then authorize then exchange). Root cause: stale cached state in mcp-remote no longer matches server state

v1.fact.sse_stale_session_self_heal = S16-3c: SseClient now tracks connectedAt + lastWriteOkAt. Stale scanner runs every SSE_STALE_SCAN_INTERVAL_MS (default 60s). Three eviction paths: (1) destroyed socket cleanup, (2) MAX_SESSION_AGE hard cap sends reconnect event with tree head then evicts, (3) IDLE_THRESHOLD probe write detects half-open sockets. Env vars: MMPM_SSE_MAX_SESSION_AGE_MS (4hr), MMPM_SSE_IDLE_THRESHOLD_MS (10min), MMPM_SSE_STALE_SCAN_MS (60s). All timers .unref() for graceful shutdown.

v1.fact.compute_state_machines_six = Compute has 6 state machines: provisioner (droplet lifecycle), substrate-provisioner (Docker container lifecycle: provisioning→running→read_only→suspended→deprovisioned), destroy-worker (pending→snapshotting→deleting→completed), grace-period-worker (read_only→deprovisioned after 30 days), entitlement-expiry (consumed→expired→pause), billing-enforcer (warn at 1hr burn rate, urgent at 5min, pause at 0).

v1.fact.tagline_source_moat_is_intelligence = Marketing tagline source: 'No competitor can replicate this unless they build the same product, run it on themselves for months, and accumulate the same arc weight history. That accumulated intelligence is a moat.' Key angle: the substrate gets smarter with time and customers. Network effects on operational intelligence. The 500th customer benefits from patterns learned from the first 499.

v1.fact.provisioning_journey_map_documented = Five provisioning journeys documented 2026-03-29: (1) Shared provision - free/indie/pro, (2) Dedicated provision - team/enterprise from DO snapshot, (3) Dedicated→Shared downgrade with data migration, (4) Delete substrate with 30-day grace period, (5) Admin migrate dedicated droplet to shared. Six reusable patterns: idempotent queue step, timeout-aware polling, billing-safe failure, atomic Traefik switch, step state machine in DB, health check protocol.

v1.fact.tier_change_export_import_pattern = Tier change data migration: pause writes on old substrate (write_mode=draining), GET /api/export NDJSON on old container (30s timeout), POST /api/import to new container, verify old.atom_count==new.atom_count, atomic Traefik switch, restore writes on new. Rollback: if count mismatch or route switch fails, stop new container, restore writes to old. MMPM needs export+import endpoints.

v1.procedure.compute_deploy_hard_restart = When pm2 reload fails to pick up new code on memory.kiwi (symptom: new routes return 'Cannot GET'), SSH in and do: pm2 stop all && pm2 delete all && pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production && pm2 save. This forces fresh process creation and eliminates tsx/module caching.

v1.fact.compute_arch_audit_findings_status_2026_04_11_src_research = Verified all 6 audit findings from compute_arch_audit_findings against current code on 2026-04-11. Port allocation race (CRITICAL): CLOSED — Sprint A rewrote allocatePort to use substrate_port_pool with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, cooling period, leak reconciler (migrations 058/059). Unbounded Maps in billing-enforcer (CRITICAL): CLOSED — pruneStaleEntries called at top of every enforce(), WARNING_TTL_MS 2h, CAP_TTL_MS 2h 15m, mapSizes() exported for tests. Polling jitter (MEDIUM): CLOSED — all 10 workers import jitteredDelay. SSH StrictHostKeyChecking (HIGH): milder than reported — no file uses =no. Three workers explicitly set accept-new (ssh-ready, substrate-telemetry, key-rotation-deps). Three (tier-change-worker, substrate-provisioner, substrate-destroyer) omit the option and rely on OS default. Fix = add '-o', 'StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new' to three SSH_OPTS arrays. Rate limiter multi-instance (MEDIUM): PARTIAL — InMemoryRateLimitStore is pluggable via RateLimitStore interface (SEC-RD03 note) but no Redis backend. Not blocking single-host launch. Tier change orphan no grace period (HIGH): OPEN — tier-change-service sets old substrate to read_only without writing grace_period_ends_at, so grace-period-worker never picks it up.

v1.fact.compute_substrate_status_enum = Substrate status source of truth = status enum column. v1.procedure.never_silent_block_user = Any business-logic refusal (tier cap, capacity, preflight failure, missing prerequisite, auth mismatch, rate limit, quota exhaustion, entitlement denial) MUST surface to the actor with: (a) that they were blocked, (b) specific reason in plain language, (c) one concrete suggested next action. The surface MUST be legible to BOTH humans AND AI agents: UI renders a toast/banner/inline message, AND the API response body carries a machine-parseable shape (error_code + human_message + next_action + optional remediation_url). Silent 500s, silent redirects, dropped response fields, and generic Error text are banned. Applies across signup, checkout, dashboard, /admin, MCP claim-key, key rotation, and all substrate lifecycle endpoints. Every such path ships with a test asserting BOTH the user-visible message AND the machine-readable response shape.

v1.task.silent_block_ux_audit_pre_launch = Audit all business-logic refusal paths across mmpm-website and parametric-memory-compute before 2026-04-25 launch. Known sites to fix: (1) signup client drops checkoutUrl from POST /api/v1/signup response causing pending_payment dead-end (L-1 in JOURNEY-REVIEW-SIGNUP-CHECKOUT.md, fact v1.fact.signup_client_discards_checkout_url_dt_2026_04_17), (2) starter cap preflight DB trigger returns silent 500 (followup #22), (3) checkout UI does not surface tier-block reason when tier is full (followup #23). Grep sweep for 5xx returns and silent redirect patterns across both repos. Each fix ships with a test asserting user-visible message plus machine-readable response shape. Linked procedure: v1.procedure.never_silent_block_user.

v1.fact.phase_2_oauth_bridge_transport_hmac = Phase 2 OAuth website↔compute bridge uses HMAC-SHA256 (not mTLS). Compute is already built for HMAC (src/middleware/bridge-auth.ts). mTLS is an additive future ADR flagged in ADR-003 consequences. Moving to mTLS now = rebuild. Decision 2026-04-20.

v1.fact.phase_2_oauth_github_downstream_creds_not_persisted = Phase 2 OAuth does NOT persist any provider-issued bearers from GitHub after sign-in. Decision 2026-04-20.

v1.procedure.oauth_evidence_verifier_contract — createDefaultEvidenceVerifier in src/services/oauth-service.ts. Google path: jose.jwtVerify against Google JWKS with issuer∈{https://accounts.google.com, accounts.google.com}, audience=googleClientId, algorithms=['RS256'], requiredClaims=['sub','email','email_verified'], then cross-checks email_verified===true, sub===claims.providerSub, case-insensitive email match. GitHub path: GET /user (401→github_token_revoked; id.toString() must===claims.providerSub), then GET /user/emails (primary+verified email must match claims.email case-insensitively). Defence-in-depth: rejects google-id-token evidence when claims.provider!=='google' and vice-versa. Gate runs BEFORE the emailVerified check in both signinOrLinkByVerifiedClaims and linkIdentityToAccount.

v1.fact.oauth_rejection_reasons_wire_parity — website src/lib/auth/providers/types.ts REJECTION_REASONS (7 items including evidence_invalid) must stay in lockstep with compute src/services/oauth-service.ts RejectionCode union. Parity enforced by src/lib/auth/providers/types.test.ts on the website side. If the two drift, the website will silently swallow rejection reasons compute emits (or vice-versa) and audit rows become misleading.

v1.fact.google_oauth_client_id_boot_invariant — compute src/config.ts:157 throws at module-load when authOauthEnabled===true && googleOauthClientId.length===0. Fail-closed: prevents silently accepting any Google id_token regardless of aud. Value must match the website's GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID exactly — mismatch means every Google signin rejects with evidence_invalid at the aud check. Invariant short-circuits when authOauthEnabled=false so dev environments with OAuth disabled boot fine without the env var.

v1.fact.eu_ai_act_article_12_enforcement_2_aug_2026_unless_omnibus_passes = EU AI Act high-risk provisions (Art. 12 record-keeping, Art. 13 transparency, Art. 19 provider 6mo retention, Art. 26(6) deployer 6mo retention) become enforceable 2 Aug 2026. Digital Omnibus on AI proposes pushing standalone high-risk to 2 Dec 2027 and embedded high-risk to 2 Aug 2028; trilogue agreement targeted late April 2026 but as of 2026-04-26 nothing has passed. Penalties up to €15M or 3% worldwide turnover (Art. 99). Article 12 text does NOT mandate cryptographic/tamper-proof/Merkle — that framing comes from commentary (FireTail, Help Net Security). prEN 18229-1 and ISO/IEC DIS 24970 still in development; no harmonised standard yet means no presumption of conformity.

v1.procedure.website_testid_registration_required = USER CORRECTION (2026-04-27): Every new data-testid added to the mmpm-website source must be registered in docs/DUAL-ACCESSIBILITY.md in the SAME change. The CI guard scripts/check-testids.mjs walks src/, extracts every data-testid literal, and fails if any is not registered (HARD FAIL on drift). The registry leads source — pre-registration is permitted, source-without-registry is forbidden. When you add a testid: (1) put it in a markdown table in docs/DUAL-ACCESSIBILITY.md under the surface section that owns the file, (2) format is `| \`testid-name\` | Element |`, (3) backtick-wrapped, kebab-case, ≥2 hyphen-segments. Run `node scripts/check-testids.mjs` to confirm pass before committing. This rule has bitten Claude in the closed-source migration sprint (2026-W18) — 8 testids were emitted in src/app/copyright/page.tsx + src/components/ui/SiteFooter.tsx without registration, blocking preflight.

v1.fact.pending_payment_window_brief = pending_payment substrate status is a brief window (seconds, not minutes) between substrate row INSERT at checkout-session creation and the subscription.created webhook firing. Confirmed via ops timeline for acct_e08f2188 on 2026-04-27: checkout_session_created, subscription_created, and substrate_provisioned all at 02:19 UTC, provisioning took 2s. The substrate row's updated_at can be much later (e.g. 02:44) due to subsequent subscription-status syncs or health probes — NOT due to a long pending_payment window.

v1.procedure.sprint_item_metadata_required = Every sprint item must carry: ID, status, effort (XS/S/M/L/XL with LOC band), risk, blast radius, depends/blocks, files, footgun mitigated (if any), tags, acceptance criteria, test plan, rollback plan. Without this metadata an item is a TODO not a contracted change.

v1.fact.b4_fixed_starter_added_to_container_limits_2026_05_03 = B4 (admin shows Free-tier limits for Starter customers) is FIXED as of 2026-05-03. Resolution: (a) added `starter` row to all 4 worker container-limit maps (services/tier-change-service.ts CONTAINER_LIMITS, workers/mock-tier-change-worker.ts TIER_LIMITS, workers/substrate-provisioner.ts CONTAINER_LIMITS, workers/mock-substrate-provisioner.ts CONTAINER_LIMITS) with values matching SUBSTRATE_TIERS.starter.limits (1000/200/100 MB); (b) made each map an export const so the test can import; (c) appended a 12-assertion describe block to tests/unit/tier-consistency.test.ts asserting all 4 maps have all 5 canonical tiers and starter values match canonical; (d) shipped migration 084_backfill-substrate-limits-from-tier.sql to backfill any drifted denormalised columns (idempotent, no-op DOWN per migration 038 convention, 1 row affected on dev `quiet-summit`, 0 on prod). Side-fix: relaxed an over-strict regex in tests/unit/substrate-provisioner-stats-key.test.ts:124 that demanded single-line composition `encryptStatsKey(generateStatsReadKey())`; the mock-substrate-provisioner needs the intermediate `statsReadKeyRaw` because it also POSTs the raw key to /control/register-stats-key (line ~317). New test asserts semantic constraint: decl exists + appears before INSERT. Verified by independent sonnet verifier (PASS — SHIP IT). Preflight clean (3820/3820). Committed.

v1.event.s12_b4_shipped_2026_05_03 = S1.2 B4 fix shipped 2026-05-03. Day 1 of Sprint 1 closed at half-day target. Pre-existing other-sprint working-tree changes (stats-read-key TDD work in substrate-provisioner.ts and mock-substrate-provisioner.ts) remain in working tree per user's `we will sort at the end of the sprint` direction; the failing test from that sprint was fixed inline rather than stashed (per user's `i dont commit when there are failing tests, so this is our problem` correction). Sprint 1 now ahead of schedule: S0.3 (CI guards) is the Day 1-2 work, scheduled to start next.

v1.procedure.use_bash_inline_when_edit_tool_eperm_2026_05_03 = HARD RULE (user correction 2026-05-03): when Read/Edit/Write tools EPERM on a user file path, do the edit INLINE via bash (workspace tool), never hand the user a python heredoc or shell script to run. The user's flow is one-action-at-a-time and they've explicitly said handing them scripts wastes time + tokens. Bash from my sandbox has write access to the mounted user folders even where Read/Edit do not. Examples observed 2026-05-03: Read EPERM on parametric-memory-compute/DECISIONS.md, mmpm-website/docs/DUAL-ACCESSIBILITY.md, mmpm-website/src/app/api/billing/upgrade-options/route.test.ts — all editable via bash python heredoc or sed. Action: when Edit returns EPERM, immediately retry via `mcp__workspace__bash` with a python or sed inline edit, do NOT hand a script to the user. Only ask the user to run the edit if bash also fails.

v1.event.full_preflight_3871_passed_dt_2026_05_04 = Full preflight passed 2026-05-04: typecheck ✓, unit tests 1476 ✓, DB tests 1252 ✓, integration tests 1002 ✓, property tests 4 ✓, security tests 137 ✓ = 3871 / 3871 across all five gates. Verifies the complete sprint: Pro=dedicated config flip, mock dedicated branch, isMain worker guards, expectedTransitionType helper refactor (13 consumer-test sites), Branch B fall-through warning + failure mark, stale memory tombstoned. Worktree contains 8 modified + 5 new files (4 test files + 1 helper).

v1.fact.research_substrate_masterRoot_spec_violation_2026_05_09 = Production research substrate (mmpm.co.nz, treeVersion=2 at audit time) emits a snapshot tree.masterRoot that does NOT equal MerkleKernel.rootOfHashes(shardRootBufs) per spec SPEC-SNAPSHOT.md §6.4 Check B sub-check 4. Audit ran 2026-05-09 via npm run audit:research; A9 reported stored=d1ccd2427cf01c74... computed=e11a9046cef973ad.... Other Merkle sub-checks (shardRoots, edgesRoot, auditLogRoot) all PASS; only masterRoot diverges. Implies production's master tree commits to MORE than shardRoots OR uses a different ordering/recipe. Either the spec needs to be widened to describe production behaviour OR production needs to be brought into line with the spec. Investigation owed; do not assume which side is correct without reading src/master.ts, src/orchestrator.ts, and the spec §2.6 master-tree definition.

v1.procedure.bash_heredoc_workaround_eperm_mount = When Read/Write/Edit file tools EPERM on a mounted folder but the bash sandbox CAN read+write the same paths, the host->sandbox mount only exposes the folder to the Linux uid, not the path the file tools resolve through. Workaround: edit via `cat <<'EOF' > /sessions/<id>/mnt/<repo>/<path>` heredocs in mcp__workspace__bash. Verify with a `.bash-writetest` probe first; verified working 2026-05-11 on mmpm-cli. Final on-disk diff is identical, user still reviews via git on their host.

v1.event.cli_snapshot_v1_canonical_fix_2026_05_11 = mmpm-cli snapshot check rewritten to canonical SnapshotV1 (Session 2026-05-11). New src/lib/snapshot-types.ts mirrors canonical types + isSnapshotV1Shape + looksLikeLegacyFlatShape. src/checks/snapshot.ts now reads tree.* nested fields, checks formatVersion=='1.0.0', validates Ed25519 envelope (alg/kid/sig/publicKey/keyUri), drops signedPayloadSha256. Tests rewritten with canonical fixture + legacy-flat regression guard + optional real-test-vector drift guard. 71/71 tests green. Unblocks rollout of vault-signing customers (4 remaining on old compose).

v1.event.s_ac_audit_rollout_5_customer_substrates_complete_dt_2026_05_11 = S-AC (substrate-audit-completeness) rollout to all 5 existing pre-S-AC customer substrates completed 2026-05-11. Slugs: hidden-edge, keystone-moss, rugged-depot, sunlit-mirror, verdant-lagoon. Every Ed25519-signed snapshot from these substrates now embeds .exporter.host=<slug>.droplet-mcp.nz. Verified via authenticated POST /admin/export-snapshot from inside each customer's nginx, parsed via jq on the host. All 5 containers Healthy after force-recreate (~17-18s each). 4 substrates had 8-key compose insert + recreate; keystone-moss already had compose patched from prior partial work, just needed recreate.

edges.createdBy: UNIQUE(provider,provider_sub), UNIQUE(account_id,provider), UNIQUE(LOWER(email_at_link)) WHERE verified_at IS NOT NULL. Plus sibling table account_identity_audit for link/unlink/auto_link actions.

edges.target: do not reintroduce sudo_sessions for OAuth link/unlink. Instead require a fresh login action within the last 5 minutes before any identity mutation. Cheaper, uses existing primitives, no new table. Audit table account_identity_audit records every link/unlink/auto_link.

magic-link via Resend, compute owns accounts + magic_link_tokens + auth_sessions (migrations 001, 022). Website holds mmpm_session cookie (HttpOnly;Secure;SameSite=Lax;30d), proxies Bearer to compute. Session write-throttled (SEC-4) to refresh only in last 15d of 30d TTL. Dead code

edges.target: compute ops substrate is NOT just email notifications. Full scope includes
edges.createdBy: billing assistance (help users understand charges, manage spend caps, understand tier options, troubleshoot payment), lifecycle management (provisioning, suspension, reactivation, destruction), proactive billing guidance (infer when user needs help before they ask), spend optimisation recommendations, tier upgrade/downgrade guidance. The substrate encodes billing intelligence, not just notification timing.

edges.target: account atoms use hashed ID (one-way, never reversible from substrate), pattern
edges.createdBy: v1.event.acct_{hash}_{signal}_{date}. Population-level learned patterns stored as v1.fact.pattern.*. Lifecycle state as v1.state.acct_{hash}.lifecycle. Privacy boundary

edges.target: ops-writer service hooks into 6 state machine transitions (provisioner, substrate-provisioner, destroy-worker, grace-period, entitlement-expiry, billing-enforcer), writes non-PII operational atoms per account (hashed ID), inference agent bootstraps substrate to decide intervention timing/content. Feedback loop trains Markov arcs from outcomes. Phase 1

ops-writer service hooks into 6 state machine transitions (provisioner, substrate-provisioner, destroy-worker, grace-period, entitlement-expiry, billing-enforcer), writes non-PII operational atoms per account (hashed ID), inference agent bootstraps substrate to decide intervention timing/content. Feedback loop trains Markov arcs from outcomes. Phase 1

edges.createdBy: commercial billing, provisioning, DigitalOcean workers, Stripe, Resend email, credit system, mmpm-compute service, PM2 processes

provisioning→running→read_only→suspended→deprovisioned), destroy-worker (pending→snapshotting→deleting→completed), grace-period-worker (read_only→deprovisioned after 30 days), entitlement-expiry (consumed→expired→pause), billing-enforcer (warn at 1hr burn rate, urgent at 5min, pause at 0).

edges.source: v1.fact.compute_substrate_status_enum = Substrate status source of truth = status enum column. Values
edges.target: provisioning, pending_payment, running, read_only, suspended, deprovisioned, destroyed, provision_failed, deprovisioning. Decommissioned = status IN ('deprovisioned','destroyed','provision_failed'). No separate deleted_at column on substrates. grace_period_ends_at governs read_only window and reactivation eligibility.

v1.fact.compute_two_disconnected_provisioning_models = init.sql + migrations 001-026 built droplet model (instances table, entitlements, one-time payments). Migration 029 created separate substrates table with own provision queue and subscription billing. substrate-provisioner.ts writes to instances table but webhook writes to substrates table — completely disconnected. Neither pipeline is complete end-to-end.

edges.target: extend existing polling response shapes, do NOT create generic substrate_events table or SSE/websocket. /api/v1/substrates/
edges.createdBy: slug (polled 10s) gains provisioning.phase, provisioning.errorMessage, tierChangeInProgress, gracePeriodEndsAt, readOnlyReason. /api/billing/status (polled 10s, 60s cache) gains paymentFailureDetails (attemptCount+nextAttemptAt+amountCents), invoiceUpcomingAt, dispute object. DB schema already captures signals in billing_events and queue rows (migrations 009 027 049 061 070 072 verified) - gap is exposure not collection.

edges.source: v1.fact.concurrent_test_fix_batched_io = Concurrent stress test fixed by three production code changes
edges.target: (1) throttle PPM persistence to 30s intervals, (2) batch access metrics into single LevelDB batch write during commit, (3) batch access log entries into same flush. Eliminates 3 individual LevelDB writes per reader access call.
per-customer container pair, isolated bridge network, stop_grace_period 30s, read_only filesystem, resource limits per tier.

edges.target: slug (mono), tier badge, status badge (color-coded), created date. Click navigates to /admin?slug=X. Add Substrate CTA links to /pricing. Polls /api/substrates every 10s. Post-checkout banner polls every 2s for 60s. Account-level BillingWidget at top.

edges.target: data-testid kebab-case prefixed by surface (nav-*, dashboard-*, admin-*, checkout-*), verb+object for actions (dashboard-add-substrate, admin-rotate-key). aria-label matches visible text or clarifies intent for icon-only buttons. Every UI action has an API equivalent listed in /.well-known/actions.json with zod-validated shape. Action JSON-LD (schema.org Action types) on public pages. CI guards fail PRs that remove testids or break actions manifest (scripts/check-testids.mjs, scripts/check-actions-manifest.mjs). Doc

v1.fact.finding_stub_atoms_skip_edges = Atoms stored without a value (key-only stubs) appear in semantic search results but the server skips edge writes for them. 19 edges skipped across two checkpoint calls. Stubs are real index entries but may lack a full atom record for edge attachment. Affects

v1.fact.force_directed_graph_for_knowledge_visual = Physics simulation (spring attraction + Coulomb repulsion) converges to equilibrium. d3-force-3d and 3d-force-graph libraries available. Medium graphs (50-500 nodes) show best quality. Symmetry and uniform edge length emerge naturally. Perfect for ~1000 atoms visualization.

v1.fact.knowledge_scientific_viz_key_distinction = Existing viz is simulation (force physics approximates structure, position emergent). Scientific viz is projection (measured quantities mapped directly to visual channels — Poincare embeddings to position, timestamps to depth, Markov weights to flow vectors). No intermediary physics model. Shows substrate as mathematical object with geometry, dynamics, and history.

v1.fact.knowledge_scientific_viz_three_paradigms = Scientific /knowledge viz uses three layered paradigms
edges.target: (1) Topographic Landscape — Poincare positions as 2D ground plane, Gaussian KDE for elevation, atom type colors terrain. (2) Temporal Stratigraphy — Z-depth = log(age), translucent strata bands at week/month/quarter, time scrubber for excavation. (3) Markov Flow Field — vector field from weighted sum of arc directions, instanced arrow particles advecting along probability streamlines.

edges.source: v1.fact.launch_gate_trigger_blocks_substrates = enforce_launch_gate trigger on instances table requires entitlement with matching droplet_size. Substrates use subscriptions not entitlements and have no droplet_size. Trigger would block every substrate INSERT. Solution
edges.target: substrates table is separate so trigger doesnt fire.

v1.fact.mmpm_community_detection_impl = community_detection.ts uses union-find connected components (not graphology-louvain which is blocked in sandbox). Hub atom name = v1.other.community_auto_<type>_<djb2hash>. Confidence 0.8. Idempotent by hash. graphology can be swapped in later.

All run with Testcontainers+mock-do-server, no real DO calls.

edges.createdBy: commercial billing, provisioning, DigitalOcean workers, Stripe, Resend email, credit system, mmpm-compute service, PM2 processes

edges.target: EVERY customer gets isolated substrate (own container pair, own LevelDB volume, own Merkle tree root). No shared database or multi-tenant substrate. One exploit does not leak other customers' memories. Compliance requirement for healthcare/finance/legal sectors. Scales via automation not consolidation.
accounts, instances, queues (provision/destroy/traefik_config), entitlements, ledger tables (usage, billing_events), auth tables (magic_link_tokens, auth_sessions), snapshots table

(1) DELETE /v2/droplets/{id} returns 404 if already gone but doRequest throws on 404 indistinguishable from 500. (2) No 429 backoff anywhere - rate limit causes immediate failure. (3) Snapshot match by name is fragile if snapshot still propagating. (4) GET /v2/droplets?tag_name=X not yet used but critical for idempotency.

edges.source: v1.fact.mmpm_compute_entitlement_gate = launch_gate DB trigger (AFTER INSERT on instances)
edges.target: checks account.status=active, finds available entitlement with matching droplet_size and period_end>now(), atomically sets entitlement.status=consumed+instance_id. RAISE EXCEPTION rolls back entire TX if no entitlement. FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED prevents race.

edges.target: If Phase 1 POST to DO succeeds but HTTP response is lost, provisioner never records droplet_id. Queue row goes failed, reaper resets to pending, second droplet created. Phantom droplets bill indefinitely. Fix

compute-server (Express API, port 3100), compute-provisioner (DO droplet creation, holds DO write token), compute-traefik (SSL registration outbox worker), compute-destroyer (snapshot+delete droplets, holds DO write token), compute-reaper (resets stuck queue rows every 5min)

edges.target: Ops substrate atoms must be narrative observations, not structured event records. Each atom reads like a case note written by a knowledgeable operator
edges.createdBy: includes the immediate signal PLUS full account context (tier, customer age, recent events, usage levels, warning history, intervention outcomes). Atoms are queryable against any future question because they contain semantic richness, not predetermined fields. No event bus, no typed channels — one observer service that gathers context from Postgres and composes natural language atoms.

edges.source: v1.fact.saas_plan_billing_intelligence_scope = The ops substrate is NOT just notification optimisation. Full scope
edges.target: billing assistance (help users understand charges), spend guidance (cap recommendations), lifecycle management (provisioning through destruction), tier recommendations (usage-driven upgrade suggestions), proactive intervention (infer when user needs help before they ask), churn prevention (pattern-matched early warning), and onboarding optimisation (activation email timing from first-session behaviour). The notification service is one consumer. The inference agent, dashboard, and future AI-assisted support are others.

edges.source: v1.fact.substrate_billing_model_subscription_based = Substrate billing uses Stripe subscriptions (not one-time payments like compute). One subscription = one substrate, billed monthly. Free tier provisioned at signup without charge. Paid tiers go through Stripe Checkout. Cancellation triggers 30-day read-only grace period then deprovision. Overages metered at sub-cent per atom above tier limit via Stripe usage records.

edges.target: (a) RecoveryRow SELECT pulls spq.hosting_model/droplet_id/droplet_ip + LEFT JOIN compute_hosts for ch.ssh_target/ip_address — dedicated recovery uses the correct host SSH target; (b) explicit Phase 0 (Branch A) recovery for queue-only crashes — verifies droplet via getDropletStatus, resets to pending if alive or marks failed if gone; (c) Phase 1-4 (Branch B) for substrate-row-exists crashes; (d) NEW 2026-05-04

edges.target: 'No competitor can replicate this unless they build the same product, run it on themselves for months, and accumulate the same arc weight history. That accumulated intelligence is a moat.' Key angle
edges.createdBy: the substrate gets smarter with time and customers. Network effects on operational intelligence. The 500th customer benefits from patterns learned from the first 499.

edges.target: 'The product IS the proof, and the proof IS the product.' Core narrative
edges.createdBy: we don't sell a theoretical capability, we sell exactly what we run. When a prospect asks 'does this work?' the answer is 'we run our entire operation on it — billing, lifecycle management, churn prevention, onboarding optimisation.' The demo is your actual ops dashboard powered by your actual substrate trained on your actual data. Competitors can't replicate this without building the same product AND running it on themselves for months. The accumulated arc weight history is a moat.

edges.target: 'We don't use rule-based operations management. We use Parametric Memory.' Contrast angle
edges.createdBy: traditional SaaS runs on hardcoded thresholds, cron jobs, and human ops staff. MMPM-powered SaaS runs on learned knowledge that improves itself. No ops headcount scaling needed.

edges.source: v1.fact.telemetry_three_signals = Capacity gating uses three signals
edges.target: (1) docker stats via SSH per host every 5min — actual container RAM fill, gate at 70%. (2) DO Monitoring API every 30min — host-level mem/cpu/disk, gate mem at 80% disk at 85%. (3) counter-based fill current_tenants/max_tenants gate at 75%. Any signal triggers draining status. Re-open requires counter < 65% AND no resource signal firing.

edges.target: mmpm-service + mmpm-mcp Docker container pair. Only difference is WHERE containers run. Shared tiers (free/indie/pro) deploy to existing substrate host. Dedicated tiers (team/enterprise) create DO droplet first then deploy same containers. Phase 1 (acquire host) branches on tier. edges.target: M-1B was originally planned as a separate src/workers/dedicated-provisioner.ts worker with its own PM2 entry. Implementation consolidated both hosting models into a single substrate-provisioner.ts with branching only in Phase 1 (acquireHost). Phases 2-4 (container deploy, health check, Traefik registration) are identical code regardless of hosting model — only difference is SSH target. This is simpler
edges.createdBy: halves PM2 process count, eliminates cross-worker coordination, and the milestone doc should be updated to reflect this is the preferred architecture.

Simpler architecture, halves PM2 process count, eliminates cross-worker coordination. Milestone doc now reflects this is preferred.

edges.source: v1.fact.viz_rendering_approach = Particle-based rendering with InstancedMesh for 1-2 draw calls across 5000 atoms. TSL shaders auto-compile to GLSL or WGSL. WebGPU compute handles pulse timing and position physics. UnrealBloomPass for glow. Zustand for state. WebSocket for real-time MMPM events. Bundle approx 300KB gzipped.

v1.fact.website_dashboard_vs_admin_roles = /dashboard = read-only glance grid showing ALL substrates (active + decommissioned), with live health/SSL/tier/https badges on active ones only, status-only display for decommissioned. No management actions anywhere. /admin = administrative list of all substrates (grouped or filtered active vs decommissioned). Both dashboard cards and admin list rows navigate to /admin/[slug] on click. /admin/[slug] = detail page with all management actions (usage, rotate key, upgrade tier, cancel, reactivate, deprovision, MCP server block).

edges.source: v1.procedure.compute_deploy_hard_restart = When pm2 reload fails to pick up new code on memory.kiwi (symptom
edges.target: new routes return 'Cannot GET'), SSH in and do
edges.createdBy: pm2 stop all && pm2 delete all && pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --env production && pm2 save. This forces fresh process creation and eliminates tsx/module caching.
