v1.procedure.live_association_agent_prompt = Haiku subagent spawned after every session_checkpoint with new atoms. Finds cross-domain bridges via memory_associate, writes references edges (confidence 0.4) via session_checkpoint with ONLY edges (no atoms, no taskContext). Max 5 edges per run. Skips bootstrap, session-end checkpoint, tombstone-only, train-only checkpoints. Cost <500 tokens per pulse.

edges.source: v1.procedure.mmpm_compute_init_sql_canonical = init.sql at mmpm-compute root is the canonical single-file DB schema. All table changes must be reflected in init.sql so the project is portable and the DB can be recreated anywhere with psql -f init.sql. Migrations folder is for incremental dev changes but init.sql is the source of truth.

v1.procedure.mmpm_compute_log_standards = Use LOG (ERROR/WARN/INFO) not console.log. Timestamp and level auto-added. Structured key=value for dims. ERROR logs = potential security/data bug + Page. WARN = degradation or retryable. INFO = state transitions.

edges.target: v1.<type>.<concise_snake_case> where type in (fact, state, event, relation, procedure, domain, task, other). Names under 10 tokens. No colons/periods. Jaccard-based conflict detection
edges.createdBy: last underscore = claim key, rest = conflict key. Numbered suffixes cause false positives (NEVER use).

v1.procedure.mmpm_treeversion_reset_on_restart = treeVersion resets to 1 on container restart — this is expected and confirms hitting the newly restarted container. Data is intact if LevelDB files are present and PPM trie restores correctly. Not a sign of data loss.

edges.source: v1.procedure.never_silent_block_user = Any business-logic refusal (tier cap, capacity, preflight failure, missing prerequisite, auth mismatch, rate limit, quota exhaustion, entitlement denial) MUST surface to the actor with
edges.target: (a) that they were blocked, (b) specific reason in plain language, (c) one concrete suggested next action. The surface MUST be legible to BOTH humans AND AI agents
edges.createdBy: UI renders a toast/banner/inline message, AND the API response body carries a machine-parseable shape (error_code + human_message + next_action + optional remediation_url). Silent 500s, silent redirects, dropped response fields, and generic Error text are banned. Applies across signup, checkout, dashboard, /admin, MCP claim-key, key rotation, and all substrate lifecycle endpoints. Every such path ships with a test asserting BOTH the user-visible message AND the machine-readable response shape.

edges.source: v1.procedure.nightly_orphan_wiring = After nightly recluster, haiku agent (2am+2min) wires orphans
edges.target: atoms with no member_of hub edge get assigned to closest matching hub by heuristic type. Multi-valued hubs
edges.createdBy: computed by member_of count across all atoms. Batch orphan edges and write via session_checkpoint. Cost <100 tokens.

edges.source: v1.procedure.no_separate_tier_droplet_map = There is no separate TIER_DROPLET_MAP constant in entitlement-service. Droplet size for dedicated tiers lives inline as SUBSTRATE_TIERS[tier].dedicatedDropletSize on the tier config object. To add or change droplet size for a dedicated tier, edit substrate-tier.ts only.

v1.procedure.oauth_account_linking_rule = OAuth first sign-in with verified email auto-links to existing account with same email. Sign-in path does NOT prompt for confirmation. Add-a-provider path (user already logged in) is separate, sudo-gated with oauth_link action and 5-min re-auth window. Unverified provider email is always rejected before any compute call.

edges.target: Observer injected as optional dep to service factories. Workers create opsObserver = createOpsObserver(pool) at top. All calls are fire-and-forget void — never awaited by callers.

one service (ops-observer.ts) called explicitly at each significant moment. Takes accountId + signal type + immediate data. Gathers full account context from Postgres (tier history, recent billing events, usage stats, previous warnings and outcomes). Composes a 2-4 sentence narrative atom. Writes to substrate fire-and-forget. Trains Markov arc from previous atom for same account. No event emitter, no typed bus, no rigid schema.

v1.procedure.python_replace_script_must_save_per_section_or_atomic = When using a Python heredoc to apply multi-section file edits, write each section's changes to disk INSIDE the section (per-file write_text after each modification group) rather than building all changes in memory and writing at the end. If the script crashes after partial work, the in-memory-only changes are silently lost. Cost we paid
edges.target: a tier-change-service.test.ts script crashed at an assertion mid-section, all changes lost, required full re-run. Pattern
edges.createdBy: for each file that needs edits, finish the changes for that file and immediately p.write_text(src) before moving to the next file.

v1.procedure.test_derive_consequences_from_canonical_config = When a test asserts a value that is computed from a known canonical config (e.g. SUBSTRATE_TIERS), derive the expected value from that config via a small test helper rather than hardcoding the literal. When the test asserts the canonical config itself (e.g. tier-consistency.test.ts), hardcode. Concrete trigger
edges.target: any time someone asks 'should we change tier X's hosting model', first ship a helper like expectedTransitionType(from, to) that derives the answer from SUBSTRATE_TIERS, so the next config shift updates atomically instead of requiring a scavenger hunt across N test files. Cost we just paid
edges.createdBy: 7+ hardcoded transitionType assertions across upgrade-handlers.test.ts and tier-change-service.test.ts had to be hand-updated when Pro flipped from shared to dedicated 2026-05-04. Tradeoff

v1.procedure.trial_cap_exceeded_skip_all_stripe = When a trialing subscription hits cap_exceeded, skip BOTH refund AND cancel — no Stripe calls at all. No charge has been taken so no refund needed. The subscription row is marked cap_exceeded and the trial expires naturally without converting. Deliberately does NOT cancel
edges.target: cap_exceeded row is the single source of truth.

v1.procedure.validate_substrate_via_cowork_mcp_client = Cowork (this app) can serve as an independent MCP client to validate a freshly provisioned substrate's OAuth handshake + 5-location chain without needing a separate Claude Desktop config test. The substrate's MCP server auto-registers as a Cowork connector (visible via ToolSearch under its server name, e.g. Smooth-mcp). Two reads (memory_list_atoms + memory_session_bootstrap) plus one write (session_checkpoint) plus a re-list to confirm treeVersion bump is sufficient to prove the chain intact. Stronger than Claude-Desktop-only because it exercises a second independent client implementation.

v1.procedure.verify_atoms_functional_before_proposing_deletion = Before recommending tombstone or deletion of any atom or class of atoms, verify the atom is broken (404 on access AND no search hits AND no edges) — not just non-canonical. Structural divergence from spec is not corruption. Investigate before destruction.

v1.procedure.webhook_swallow_refund_errors_return_200 = The webhook handler wraps refundAndCancelForCapExceeded in try/catch. On failure
edges.target: log error, emit substrate_cap_refund with outcome=error + errorMessage, still return 200 to Stripe. This prevents infinite webhook retries. The helper itself throws (not swallows) so unit tests can assert failure modes — same pattern as SM-47 observability-side-effects-never-convert-404-into-500.

edges.createdBy: (1) Internal event bus with typed events from all 6 state machines, (2) Account hash function for non-PII atom keys, (3) Ops writer service (event-to-atom with local buffer), (4) Feedback atom infrastructure with Markov arc training, (5) Billing context enrichment (rich state atoms per account), (6) Read-only inference agent (6h schedule, recommendations only, no autonomous action), (7) Population pattern atoms (deferred 60 days for data accumulation). 20 files changed (7 new, 13 edited). edges.target: Knowledge-First Operational Intelligence. Sprint doc at docs/sprint-ops-substrate.md.

atoms: v1.fact.snapshot_v1_envelope_canonical_shape = SnapshotV1 canonical shape (mm-memory/markov-merkle-memory/src/snapshot/types.ts): formatVersion='1.0.0' at top level; tree.* NESTED (masterRoot, treeVersion, treeHeadTimestampMs, shardCount, shardRoots[], shardLeafCounts[], atomCount, edgesRoot, auditLogRoot?); exporter.{name, version, host, exportedAtMs, exportedAtIso}; atoms[]/edges[]/hubAtoms[] top-level; signature.{alg='Ed25519', kid, publicKey, publicKeyFingerprint, keyUri, sig}. NO signature.value, NO signature.keyId, NO signedPayloadSha256 — signing target is canonicalize(snapshot with signature.sig="").

v1.procedure.mirror_canonical_types_dont_redeclare_optionals = When porting a shared type (e.g. SnapshotV1) into a downstream package, mirror exactly: same field names, same required-ness, same nesting. NEVER redeclare with everything-optional — that compiles silently against the wrong shape and is exactly how mmpm-cli got the legacy flat-shape parser past code review for months. Add a source-of-truth comment naming the canonical file. Add a fixture-based test that loads a real golden file from the canonical repo (skip-if-missing so CI without the checkout still passes) — that fixture is the spec-drift regression guard.

v1.procedure.bash_heredoc_workaround_eperm_mount = When Read/Write/Edit file tools EPERM on a mounted folder but the bash sandbox CAN read+write the same paths, the host->sandbox mount only exposes the folder to the Linux uid, not the path the file tools resolve through. Workaround: edit via `cat <<'EOF' > /sessions/<id>/mnt/<repo>/<path>` heredocs in mcp__workspace__bash. Verify with a `.bash-writetest` probe first; verified working 2026-05-11 on mmpm-cli. Final on-disk diff is identical, user still reviews via git on their host.

v1.event.cli_snapshot_v1_canonical_fix_2026_05_11 = mmpm-cli snapshot check rewritten to canonical SnapshotV1 (Session 2026-05-11). New src/lib/snapshot-types.ts mirrors canonical types + isSnapshotV1Shape + looksLikeLegacyFlatShape. src/checks/snapshot.ts now reads tree.* nested fields, checks formatVersion=='1.0.0', validates Ed25519 envelope (alg/kid/sig/publicKey/keyUri), drops signedPayloadSha256. Tests rewritten with canonical fixture + legacy-flat regression guard + optional real-test-vector drift guard. 71/71 tests green. Unblocks rollout of vault-signing customers (4 remaining on old compose).

v1.state.cli_snapshot_check_canonical_v1 = mmpm-cli/src/checks/snapshot.ts on canonical SnapshotV1 shape as of 2026-05-11. Surface check only: shape + formatVersion + signature envelope. Deep crypto (Merkle reconstruction from shardRoots, edgesRoot recomputation, JWKS resolution, Ed25519 verification) still TODO — port from mm-memory/markov-merkle-memory/scripts/check-substrate-readiness.ts (A6–A16 assertions).

v1.event.s_ac_audit_rollout_5_customer_substrates_complete_dt_2026_05_11 = S-AC (substrate-audit-completeness) rollout to all 5 existing pre-S-AC customer substrates completed 2026-05-11. Slugs: hidden-edge, keystone-moss, rugged-depot, sunlit-mirror, verdant-lagoon. Every Ed25519-signed snapshot from these substrates now embeds .exporter.host=<slug>.droplet-mcp.nz. Verified via authenticated POST /admin/export-snapshot from inside each customer's nginx, parsed via jq on the host. All 5 containers Healthy after force-recreate (~17-18s each). 4 substrates had 8-key compose insert + recreate; keystone-moss already had compose patched from prior partial work, just needed recreate.

v1.fact.5_customer_substrates_audit_canonical_identity_complete_dt_2026_05_11 = All 5 existing pre-S-AC customer substrates (hidden-edge, keystone-moss, rugged-depot, sunlit-mirror, verdant-lagoon) now satisfy AI Act Article 26(6) traceability: signed snapshots embed .exporter.host=<slug>.droplet-mcp.nz (regulator-citable, slug-stamped) rather than the Docker container hash. Closed gap was 8 audit env vars (7 Vault + MMPM_EXPORTER_HOST) missing from docker-compose.yml on 4 of 5 substrates, plus MMPM_EXPORTER_HOST missing from all 5 .env files. .env backfill + sed-based compose patch + force-recreate. No regression on the 7 Vault keys (signing was already working).

v1.procedure.audit_3_layer_env_compose_container_before_substrate_ops = Before any compose-touching ops on customer substrates, run a 3-column audit per slug: .env (file present + key=value line), docker-compose.yml (environment block forwards the key as ${KEY:-}), running container (docker exec test -n "$KEY" returns 0). Reveals asymmetries — e.g. compose patched without recreate; .env value present but compose missing pass-through; partial earlier runs. Cheap, read-only, prevents acting on stale assumptions and prevents redundant work. Pattern proven on 5-substrate S-AC rollout 2026-05-11 where keystone-moss showed compose-patched-but-not-recreated state that simple act-on-assumption would have missed.

v1.procedure.bash_sed_with_tempfile_and_rollback_for_compose_yaml_patches_over_pyyaml = For production customer docker-compose.yml patches prefer bash + sed -i "/anchor/r tempfile" with .bak.<ns> backup, post-insert grep verification of all expected keys, and automatic restore on partial insert — over PyYAML safe_dump which reformats whitespace and re-quotes values even on no-op runs. sed preserves the original file byte-for-byte except for the inserted block. PyYAML patcher (patch-compose-vault-env.py) is fine for green-field provisioning and for tests; disruptive on hand-maintained customer files because diff becomes unreadable. Established 2026-05-11 during 5-customer S-AC rollout after user pushback on the patcher approach.

v1.task.mmpm_cli_v0_2_compliance_sprint_planned_2026_05_11 = Planned 3-feature sprint for mmpm-cli v0.2: (1) @parametric-memory/spec npm package extracting canonical/jcs.ts, merkle.ts, snapshot types, and exporter helpers from mm-memory so verifier and exporter share one source of truth; (2) port A1-A16 deep crypto checks from check-substrate-readiness.ts to mmpm-cli snapshot.ts via the new package; (3) per-OS data-dir default for snapshot retention (macOS ~/Library/Application Support/MMPM/snapshots, Linux XDG_DATA_HOME/mmpm/snapshots, Windows %APPDATA%/MMPM/snapshots); (4) mmpm schedule install/status/remove subcommand for daily-backup crontab on macOS/Linux and Task Scheduler XML on Windows. Held from 2026-05-11 session due to context budget (~80% used by 5-customer S-AC rollout). Next session: design @parametric-memory/spec package — public API, what moves vs stays, semver policy, CI — as a design doc, no code. Then 2-3 implementation sessions.

v1.state.mmpm_cli_local_default_save_persisted_untracked_dt_2026_05_11 = mmpm-cli local edits (default-save snapshot to cwd with --no-save and --save-dir flags, 6 new tests under 'snapshotCheck — default-save behavior') persisted to /Users/glenosborne/Documents/code/mmpm-cli/ but UNTRACKED in git (mmpm-cli is not yet a git repository per mount inspection). TypeScript typechecks cleanly in sandbox. Vitest run blocked in sandbox by rollup native-binding arch mismatch — needs npm test on Mac to confirm runtime. Files: src/checks/snapshot.ts, src/commands/verify.ts, src/commands/help.ts, tests/unit/check-snapshot.test.ts. These become the baseline for the v0.2 sprint's per-OS data-dir change.

v1.fact.sprint_mmpm_cli_audit_scheduler_doc_dt_2026_05_11 = Sprint plan for mmpm-cli v0.2 audit scheduler + compliance folder at /Users/glenosborne/Documents/code/mmpm-cli/docs/sprint-audit-scheduler.md. 8 items (S-SCH-01 to S-SCH-08) covering per-OS data-dir resolver (macOS Library, Linux XDG, Windows APPDATA), default save-dir wire-up, help-text refresh, mmpm schedule install/status/remove, Windows Task Scheduler XML emitter, cross-platform CI matrix, manual integration test plan. 3-5 session estimate. Independent of spec-package sprints.

v1.fact.sprint_spec_package_extraction_doc_dt_2026_05_11 = Sprint plan for extracting @parametric-memory/spec npm package at /Users/glenosborne/Documents/code/mm-memory/markov-merkle-memory/docs/sprints/SPRINT-SPEC-PACKAGE-EXTRACTION.md. 11 items (S-PKG-01 to S-PKG-11) covering monorepo decision, scope decision, scaffold, extraction of canonical/jcs + merkle + snapshot types + edge_schema + exporter helpers, test-vector parity, dual ESM/CJS publish, semver policy, example third-party verifier. 4-6 session estimate. Foundation for all spec-consuming work. Does NOT include consumer refactor.

v1.fact.sprint_spec_package_adoption_doc_dt_2026_05_11 = Sprint plan for adopting @parametric-memory/spec in mm-memory + mmpm-website at /Users/glenosborne/Documents/code/mm-memory/markov-merkle-memory/docs/sprints/SPRINT-SPEC-PACKAGE-ADOPTION.md. 8 items (S-ADP-01 to S-ADP-08) covering import inventory, mm-memory rewiring, audit:research rewire, audit:research:package-parity guard script, mmpm-website rewiring, predeploy:spec-drift gate with .spec-pinned-version, coordinated upgrade playbook, shim file removal. 3-5 session estimate. Depends on SPRINT-SPEC-PACKAGE-EXTRACTION shipping first.

v1.state.sprint_spec_extraction_pending_decisions = SPRINT-SPEC-PACKAGE-EXTRACTION work paused 2026-05-12 pending user decisions on three open questions: (1) S-PKG-01 package home — user leaning toward mmpm-cli/packages/spec/ as third option (not in original sprint doc which offered mm-memory monorepo vs sibling repo); (2) inside mmpm-cli, whether to move existing CLI to packages/cli/ for clean monorepo or keep flat at root; (3) dev-link strategy mm-memory uses to consume the spec — file: link, cross-repo workspaces, or publish-and-pin. S-PKG-02 (npm scope) is decided: PUBLIC @parametric-memory/spec. Claude's recommendation: develop in mmpm-cli/packages/spec/ with full monorepo (packages/cli/ + packages/spec/), hybrid dev path = file:link during S-PKG-04 through S-PKG-07 then publish 0.1.0-alpha.0 then swap mm-memory to ^0.1.0-alpha then promote to 0.1.0 by S-PKG-08. Rationale: later-split is cheap via git subtree split if the three split-triggers appear (external contributor, ownership split, release cadence divergence); separate-repo overhead unjustified for ~1400 lines until evidence demands it. Key facts surfaced: mmpm-cli has 5 src files + 5 tests, mid-build (verify.js+help.js referenced but missing); mm-memory's spec surface is 32 importing files (18 tests, 14 runtime); merkle.ts is wire-format footgun with byte-for-byte parity as hard guardrail #5. Sprint doc at markov-merkle-memory/docs/sprints/SPRINT-SPEC-PACKAGE-EXTRACTION.md.
